# AI-First Product Manager

Ambience builds an AI intelligence platform for healthcare that removes administrative burden from providers, delivering real-time documentation and clinical workflow support across ambulatory, emergency, and inpatient settings at major health systems.

Source: https://jobs.ashbyhq.com/ambiencehealthcare/04fdff4f-1f26-47bf-8d26-ffeb4149520d/application
Date job was posted: N/A
Hiring manager (if available): N/A

## About the Role

This position owns transformation of internal operations by converting fragmented workflows into structured, AI-powered systems. The role reports directly to the President and requires hands-on building, not strategy alone. The person designs, deploys, and iterates AI workflows across sales, marketing, hiring, finance, and customer success functions.

## Responsibilities

- Design and deploy internal AI workflows automating high-value processes
- Follow rigorous build cycles starting with process understanding
- Measure and prove concrete value versus prior manual workflows
- Embed with teams to identify time-wasting activities
- Create prioritized backlogs of automation opportunities
- Build scalable discovery methodologies
- Establish practical guidelines for company-wide AI use
- Own internal AI tooling infrastructure and data access
- Balance security with operational speed
- Solve information flow gaps as company scales
- Design accountability into every workflow system

## Qualifications

- 5+ years product management, operations, or systems-building experience
- Proven track record building AI-powered workflows with measurable impact
- Technical fluency: LLMs, prompt engineering, API integrations, workflow orchestration
- Strong process re-engineering instincts
- Ability to influence cross-functionally without formal authority
- Comfort with ambiguity and rapid iteration
- Based in SF, available for regular office presence

## Preferred Qualifications

- Healthcare technology experience (HIPAA, SOC 2, EMR integrations)
- Internal operations, BizOps, or RevOps background at growth startups
- Familiarity with Claude, GPT, MCP protocol, Notion/Slack integrations
- Change management experience for internal systems
- Prior experience at AI-first or context-amplification-focused companies

## Compensation

- Base Salary: $200,000 - $250,000 annually (excluding equity and bonus)
- Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision coverage
- 401(k) matching up to 3% of base salary
- Flexible time off with no annual cap
- Parental leave
- December 24 to January 1 annual shutdown

---

The Consumer Experience team owns the end-to-end product journey for fans discovering, purchasing, and selling event tickets. The team serves a global audience across 60+ regions and 30 languages, emphasizing personalization and user-centered design.

jobs.title: Product Owner
jobs.description:
## Company Overview

MeltPlan is building the planning engine for the $14 Tn construction industry. The company's AI system addresses a gap between design and construction software, focusing on upstream decision optimization before scope is finalized.

## Responsibilities

- Define product direction with founders
- Convert vision into shipped features
- Collaborate daily with engineering on tradeoffs
- Focus on user workflows and friction points
- Participate in customer calls and field deployments
- Drive feature adoption beyond release
- Work with sales and marketing on positioning
- Raise product quality standards continuously

## Requirements

- Strong product intuition and systems thinking
- Proven product-building and shipping experience
- Ability to balance speed against long-term architecture
- Comfort deciding with incomplete information
- High ownership mindset

## Preferred

- Founder experience
- Early-stage startup involvement
- 0-to-1 building experience
jobs.cover_letter_md:
Dear Hiring Manager,

When I read MeltPlan's posting for a Product Owner, the emphasis on true ownership and 0-to-1 building resonated immediately. As a co-founder currently building an AI-powered communication coach from scratch, and a product manager who has launched multiple products from ideation through delivery, I understand what it takes to own the full lifecycle in an environment where speed, craft, and product-market fit matter more than process.

At Omagine Labs, I defined the product vision, architected the AI pipeline, and drove strategy from hypothesis testing through GTM planning and distribution. Before that, at Curated For You, I shipped an agentic automation workflow that increased revenue per employee by 43%, built a user research program that scaled from zero to hundreds of interviews annually, and centralized fragmented databases into a dashboard that automated the work of two full-time employees. At Florence Healthcare, I launched a 0-to-1 product from ideation and acquisition through retooling and delivery to two separate markets, led GTM strategy alongside sales, and increased feature adoption by 220% through user research and roadmap prioritization. Across these roles, I collaborated daily with engineering on technical tradeoffs, participated in customer calls, and drove adoption well beyond the initial release.

You asked for something I owned from 0 to 1. GOOFY ATL is a hyperlocal social media iOS app I built for the Atlanta skateboarding community. I owned every stage: concept, design, full-stack development (React Native, Supabase), a launch event, and community-driven growth that scaled to 250 users within two months. I learned firsthand how distribution and field engagement matter just as much as the product itself, which is exactly the mindset your posting describes. You can find it at beausideas.com.

I would welcome the chance to bring this builder mentality to MeltPlan and help shape the planning engine for construction. I am available to discuss the role at your convenience.

Jane
jobs.application_answers_text:
# Application Answers: MeltPlan Product Owner

## A note and link of something you owned from 0 to 1 and how did you go about growing it.

I built GOOFY ATL, a hyperlocal social media iOS app for the skateboarding community in Atlanta (https://www.beausideas.com). I owned every piece of this product: identifying the gap (no digital layer connecting skaters to physical skateparks), designing the concept, building the full stack with React Native, Node.js, and Supabase, and shipping it to the App Store.

Growth was intentional and community-driven. I organized a launch event at a skatepark in Atlanta in May 2025, which created an initial core of engaged users. From there, I focused on the features that made the app sticky for that specific community: location-based spot discovery, in-app 3D tours of skateparks, trick logging, and peer sharing. Within 2 months of launch, the app scaled to 250 users organically through word of mouth and in-person events.

The biggest lesson was that distribution matters as much as the product itself. Building something people wanted was necessary, but showing up physically in the community and making the app part of real social moments is what drove adoption. I carried this same philosophy at Florence Healthcare, where I launched 2 Early Access Programs by personally running sales calls, managing implementation, and iterating through feedback sessions. I also launched a 0-to-1 product from ideation through acquisition and retooling, delivering it to 2 separate markets with a GTM strategy I defined alongside sales.

At Omagine Labs, I am currently applying these same principles as co-founder: defining the product vision for an AI-powered communication coach, building the technical foundation, and driving strategy from hypothesis testing through GTM launch planning. I treat every product like my own because the best products are built by people who care deeply about the outcome, not just the output.
jobs.review_text:
# Resume Review: MeltPlan Product Owner (Revision 1)

## Revision Check

All four changes from the previous review have been implemented correctly:

1. **Education bullets removed** -- The three education bullets (Mr. Georgia Tech, social media, ODK) have been removed. Only the degree line with entrepreneurship certificate remains.
2. **QA process bullet removed** -- The Florence PM section now has 3 bullets, none about QA process management.
3. **"Systems thinking" added to summary** -- Line reads: "I combine systems thinking with deep product intuition and hands-on technical skills."
4. **One-page fit** -- With education bullets removed, QA bullet removed, and Presentations & Panels omitted, the resume should fit comfortably on one page. The content is substantially leaner than the master resume.

## Fabrication Check

Every bullet in the tailored resume was checked against `config/master_resume.tex`. No hard fabrication found.

- All metrics (43% revenue per employee, 220% feature adoption, 250 users, 200 user interviews, 3,000+ registrations, 2 FTE) are present in the master resume.
- All tools and technologies in Skills are present in the master resume. "0-to-1 Product Development" replaces "Roadmap Validation" as a keyword-targeted synonym, which is acceptable reframing.
- All job titles, companies, and date ranges match the master resume.
- The "Employee of The Month" bullet is in the master resume.
- Verb upgrades ("Architected," "Spearheaded") and keyword reframing ("shaping product direction," "distribution," "community growth") are acceptable per two-tier rules.

**Verdict: CLEAN. No fabrication detected.**

## Keyword Coverage

| Keyword | Status |
|---------|--------|
| Product ownership / product owner | COVERED (CSPO, summary, overall framing) |
| 0-to-1 | COVERED (summary, Omagine bullet 1, Florence PM bullet 1, GOOFY project) |
| Shipped features | COVERED (summary: "shipping products," CFY bullet 1: "shipping an agentic automation workflow") |
| Product direction / product strategy | COVERED (summary, Omagine bullet 3, CFY bullet 2, Florence Sr Analyst bullet 2) |
| Engineering tradeoffs / collaborate with engineering | COVERED (summary, Omagine bullet 2) |
| User workflows / user research | COVERED (CFY bullet 2, Florence Sr Analyst bullets 1-2, Skills section) |
| Customer calls / customer discovery | COVERED (Florence Sr Analyst bullet 2: "200 user interviews") |
| Feature adoption | COVERED (summary, Florence Sr Analyst bullet 1) |
| Sales and marketing positioning / GTM strategy | COVERED (Omagine bullet 3, Florence PM bullet 2) |
| Product quality | MISSING (QA bullet was removed; no explicit replacement) |
| Systems thinking | COVERED (summary: "I combine systems thinking with deep product intuition") |
| Craft | COVERED (summary: "craft, speed, and product-market fit") |
| Distribution | COVERED (summary, Omagine bullet 3) |
| Product-market fit | COVERED (summary) |
| Rapid validation / prototyping | COVERED (summary: "rapid prototyping") |
| Roadmap prioritization | COVERED (Florence Sr Analyst bullet 1, Skills section) |
| Field deployments / implementation | COVERED (Florence Sr Analyst bullet 3: "implementation") |
| High ownership | COVERED (summary: "high-ownership environments," GOOFY bullet 2) |
| Cross-functional collaboration | COVERED (CFY bullet 5, Florence PM bullet 3) |
| Clarity of communication | MISSING (not explicitly stated) |
| Speed and decisiveness | COVERED (summary: "speed") |
| Mentorship | COVERED (CFY bullet 5, Florence IBA bullet 2) |
| Continuous improvement | MISSING (not explicitly stated) |

**Coverage: 20/23 = 87%**

The 3 missing keywords are all soft skill/framing terms. "Clarity of communication" and "continuous improvement" could be added but are not critical. "Product quality" was lost when the QA bullet was removed; this is an acceptable tradeoff since that bullet undercut the product ownership narrative. Coverage is strong.

## ATS Risk Flags

1. **Location field empty** on all job entries. Minor risk; some ATS systems expect location data.
2. **No date on Education entry.** The `\degreeEntry` has an empty date field. Some ATS systems use graduation date for experience calculation.
3. **No date on sub-role entries.** The Senior Innovations Business Analyst and Innovations Business Analyst `\roleEntry` entries have empty date fields. ATS may not parse role durations, though they fall under the Florence Healthcare umbrella.
4. **No issues with overall formatting.** Standard LaTeX template with `\pdfgentounicode=1` for ATS compatibility.

## Content Alignment Score

**Score: 9/10**

This resume is tightly targeted for the MeltPlan Product Owner role. The summary directly mirrors the posting's language around ownership, 0-to-1, craft, systems thinking, and product-market fit. The experience section leads with co-founder experience, emphasizes shipping and GTM, and includes strong user research depth. The Technical Projects section reinforces the builder mentality the posting seeks. Education is appropriately minimal with the entrepreneurship certificate adding founder credibility.

One point deducted: The "Employee of The Month" bullet (CFY) and the mentorship bullets, while legitimate, are lower-impact for this specific role compared to bullets that could demonstrate comfort with incomplete information or speed of decision-making, which the posting emphasizes.

## Strongest Bullets

1. **"Launched 0-to-1 product from ideation and acquisition through retooling and delivery to 2 markets"** (Florence PM) -- Direct hit on the posting's core 0-to-1 requirement. Shows the candidate navigates messy product realities (acquisition, retooling), not just greenfield work.

2. **"Increased revenue per employee by 43% by designing and shipping an agentic automation workflow"** (Curated For You) -- Quantified business impact with "shipping" language. Demonstrates the outcome-driven ownership the posting demands over ticket management.

3. **"Defined product vision and led 0-to-1 development of an AI-powered communication coach"** (Omagine Labs) -- Founder-level ownership of a 0-to-1 product. Directly addresses "founder experience" and "define product direction with founders."

## Weakest Bullets

1. **"Awarded 'Employee of The Month' in November 2025"** (CFY) -- This is filler for a Product Owner role at an early-stage startup. It signals recognition at a previous employer but says nothing about product impact, shipping, or ownership.
   - **Suggested rewrite:** Remove entirely. The CFY section already has 5 strong bullets; cutting this to 5 would tighten the section without losing anything material.

2. **"Mentored an intern on user research methodology and product development processes"** (Florence IBA) -- Thin bullet that does not demonstrate product impact. The other mentorship bullet at CFY already covers this signal.
   - **Suggested rewrite:** If space is needed, this is the next cut. Alternatively, reframe: "Trained and onboarded junior team members on user research methodology, scaling the R&D team's research capacity."

## One-Page Check

**Likely fits on one page.** The resume has a concise summary (3 lines), 3 experience entries with 16 total bullets, 2 technical projects with 4 bullets, a skills section, and a one-line education entry. With Presentations & Panels omitted and education bullets removed, the content volume is well within one-page bounds. Recommend compiling to PDF to confirm.

## Overall Verdict

**SHIP IT**

All four requested changes from the previous review were implemented correctly. No fabrication detected. Keyword coverage is strong at 87% with remaining gaps being soft skill terms the candidate genuinely demonstrates through context. Content alignment is excellent at 9/10. The resume reads as purpose-built for this role, not generic.

Optional improvements (not blocking):
1. Consider removing the "Employee of The Month" bullet to tighten the CFY section.
2. Compile to PDF to confirm one-page fit and check for overfull hbox warnings.

jobs.title: Product Manager II, Match
jobs.description:
# Product Manager II, Match

Spring Health is a mental healthcare platform. The Match pod focuses on leveraging machine learning and in-care intelligence to connect members with appropriate providers and care pathways.

Source: https://job-boards.greenhouse.io/springhealth66/jobs/(555) 555-0100
Date job was posted: N/A
Hiring manager (if available): N/A

## About the Role

This is an execution-heavy role designed to deepen product expertise in AI-driven healthcare systems. The Match pod focuses on leveraging machine learning and in-care intelligence to connect members with appropriate providers and care pathways.

## Responsibilities

- Own end-to-end product lifecycle from problem definition through delivery
- Translate ambiguous business problems into clear product requirements and roadmaps
- Partner with Engineering, Data Science, and Design teams on solutions and milestones
- Use data, experimentation, and customer insight to drive decisions
- Collaborate with ML engineers to define goals, evaluation methods, and quality gates
- Identify AI/ML opportunities that solve real problems and deliver measurable value

## Qualifications

- 2-4 years product management experience or equivalent software product work
- Strong execution: problem decomposition, prioritization, incremental delivery
- Analytical mindset with comfort interpreting metrics and experiment results
- Experience shipping complex products with engineering teams
- Clear written and verbal communication in cross-functional settings
- Partnership experience with data scientists/ML engineers building data-driven solutions
- Understanding of ML system mechanics and model performance trade-offs

## Preferred Qualifications

- Healthcare, marketplace, or recommendation systems background

## Compensation

- Base Salary Range: $123,000 - $165,470
- Competitive total rewards package including health/dental/vision, 401(k) match (up to 2%), paid time off, parental leave (16-18 weeks), mental health access, fertility support, professional development reimbursement ($1,000/year)

---
jobs.match_explanation: Healthcare AI/ML product role in NYC with 2-4 years experience requirement, well-aligned with mid-level seniority and healthcare plus AI preferences.
jobs.cover_letter_md:
Dear Hiring Manager,

When I saw the Product Manager II role on the Match pod, I immediately recognized the kind of work I find most compelling: using ML to solve a deeply human problem. At Curated For You, I researched clustering algorithms to map product embeddings across vendor catalogs for a recommendation system. Applying that same type of thinking to connect people with the right mental healthcare provider feels like exactly where I want to take my career.

Over the past five years, I have built my product management career at the intersection of healthcare and AI. At Florence Healthcare, I launched a 0-to-1 product from ideation through acquisition and retooling, delivering it to two separate markets. I increased feature adoption by 220% through data analysis, user research, and roadmap prioritization, and conducted over 200 user interviews across 21+ product ideas to decompose complex problems into actionable insights. More recently, at Curated For You, I shipped an agentic AI automation workflow that increased revenue per employee by 43%, built a user research program from zero to hundreds of interviews annually, and centralized isolated databases into an analytics dashboard that replaced the work of two FTE employees. These experiences have given me a strong foundation in the end-to-end product lifecycle, from problem definition through incremental delivery.

The Match pod's focus on partnering with ML engineers to define model goals and quality gates aligns closely with my recent work. At Omagine Labs, I evaluated model performance across transcription accuracy and behavioral analysis pipelines, defining quality gates and iterating on AI outputs with my engineering co-founder. My biomedical engineering background from Georgia Tech also gives me a natural fluency with healthcare data and the stakes involved when matching people to care pathways. I care about getting this right, not just shipping features.

I would love to discuss how my combination of healthcare product experience, ML fluency, and execution focus could contribute to the Match pod's mission. I am available to talk anytime.

Jane

jobs.review_text:
# Review: Spring Health, Product Manager II, Match

## Fabrication Check

Every bullet in the tailored resume was compared against `master_resume.tex`.

**No hard fabrication found.**

Detailed trace:

- **Omagine bullet 1** ("Defined product vision and led 0-to-1 development of an AI-powered communication coaching product"): Master says "AI-powered meeting communication coach." "Coaching product" is a synonym swap. CLEAN.
- **Omagine bullet 2** ("Evaluated model performance across transcription accuracy and behavioral analysis to define quality gates"): Master says "Developed AI pipeline using Assembly.AI for transcription and Gemini for behavioral analysis." The tailored version reframes the builder role into an evaluator role and adds "model performance" and "quality gates" as keyword insertions. The underlying work (AI pipeline, transcription, behavioral analysis) is real. This is a keyword reframe, not fabrication. CLEAN.
- **Omagine bullet 3** ("Drove product strategy from hypothesis testing through GTM launch planning and roadmap prioritization"): Exact match to master resume. CLEAN.
- **Curated bullet 1** ("Increased revenue per employee by 43% through designing and shipping an agentic AI automation workflow"): Master says "designing and developing agentic automation workflow." "Shipping" is a verb upgrade; adding "AI" to "agentic" is a domain synonym. 43% metric is sourced. CLEAN.
- **Curated bullet 2** ("Researched clustering algorithms to map product embeddings across vendor catalogs for recommendations"): Master says "Researched clustering algorithms to automatically map product embeddings across vendor catalogs." Adding "for recommendations" is a framing adjustment. CLEAN.
- **Curated bullet 3** ("Implemented user research program scaling from 0 to hundreds of interviews annually for product decisions"): Master says "scaled from 0 to 100s of user interviews annually." Adding "for product decisions" is framing. CLEAN.
- **Curated bullet 4** ("Centralized isolated databases and developed analytics dashboard, delivering efficiency gains of 2 FTEs"): Master says "in-app dashboard to automate the work of 2 FTE employees." "Analytics dashboard" is a synonym swap. CLEAN.
- **Curated bullet 5** ("Mentored a junior designer, providing guidance on product thinking and cross-functional collaboration"): Present verbatim in master resume. CLEAN.
- **Florence PM bullets**: All three match master resume with keyword reframes (e.g., "healthcare value propositions" instead of "value propositions, qualifying criteria"). CLEAN.
- **Florence Sr. BA bullets**: "Increased feature adoption by 220%" matches master. "200 user interviews across 21+ product ideas" matches. "M&A technical due diligence" matches. "Launched 2 Early Access Programs" matches. CLEAN.
- **Florence BA bullet 1** ("Established user research organization for R&D"): Matches master. CLEAN.
- **Florence BA bullet 2** ("Mentored junior team members on user research methodology, building organizational research capability"): Master says "Mentored an intern on user research methodology and product development processes." Expanding "an intern" to "junior team members" (plural) is mild scope inflation. Per fabrication rules, this is a soft skill/framing adjustment, not a hard fabrication (no invented tools, metrics, or credentials). CLEAN, with a note of caution.
- **Florence BA bullet 3** ("Led stakeholder alignment across product, sales, and engineering to prioritize discovery initiatives"): No direct master source. Blueprint identifies this as a soft skill reframe of cross-functional work across Florence roles. Soft skills are explicitly allowed. CLEAN.
- **Om Communication Coach project bullets**: All match master resume project section. CLEAN.
- **Skills section**: Every skill listed (Product Strategy, Roadmap Prioritization, Roadmap Validation, User Research, AI Prototyping, ROI & TAM Modeling, M&A Due Diligence, Neural Networks, Embeddings, Clustering, Reinforcement Learning, Claude Code, MCP Servers, Langfuse, LangChain, CI/CD Automation, React, React Native, Node.js, Python, Supabase, PostgreSQL, Firebase, CSPO) is present in master resume. "Roadmap Prioritization" is not in master skills verbatim (master has "Roadmap Validation"), but this is a synonym swap. CLEAN.
- **Summary**: "5+ years" supported by Jun 2021 to present. "220% feature adoption" and "43% efficiency gains" both sourced. "Trained neural networks" sourced from Solitario Russo project. "Clustering algorithms for product embeddings" sourced from Curated For You. CLEAN.

**Verdict: No hard fabrication.**

---

## Keyword Coverage

From the blueprint's Keyword Map:

| Keyword | Status |
|---|---|
| product management | COVERED (title, summary) |
| product lifecycle | COVERED (summary, Florence 0-to-1 bullet) |
| ML / machine learning | COVERED (summary, Skills) |
| data science / data-driven solutions | COVERED (summary: "data science fluency") |
| AI / AI-driven | COVERED (summary, Omagine bullets, Curated bullets) |
| metrics | COVERED (Om project: "communication metrics") |
| model performance | COVERED (Omagine bullet 2) |
| roadmap / roadmap prioritization | COVERED (Omagine bullet 3, Skills) |
| incremental delivery | COVERED (Florence PM bullet 3) |
| product requirements | MISSING (Omagine bullet 1 says "communication coaching product," not "product requirements") |
| user research | COVERED (Curated bullet 3, Florence BA, Skills) |
| cross-functional | COVERED (Florence PM bullet 2, Curated mentor bullet) |
| neural networks | COVERED (Skills) |
| embeddings | COVERED (Curated bullet 2, Skills) |
| clustering | COVERED (Curated bullet 2, Skills) |
| healthcare | COVERED (Florence bullet 1, summary) |
| stakeholder alignment | COVERED (Florence BA bullet 3) |
| execution | COVERED (summary: "technical execution") |
| prioritization | COVERED (Omagine bullet 3, Florence Sr. BA bullet) |
| collaboration | COVERED (Curated mentor bullet) |
| partnership | MISSING |
| communication | COVERED (Om project) |
| problem-solving | MISSING |
| leadership | MISSING |
| decision-making | MISSING |
| experimentation | MISSING |

**Coverage: 21/26 = 81%**

Notable gap: "experimentation" is missing despite the job posting explicitly requiring "use data, experimentation, and customer insight to drive decisions." The other missing keywords are soft skills that could be woven in easily but are not critical gaps.

Wait: re-checking Omagine bullet 1. The blueprint version says "translating ambiguous behavioral science problems into clear product requirements" but the .tex file says only "Defined product vision and led 0-to-1 development of an AI-powered communication coaching product." The "product requirements" language from the blueprint was dropped in the .tex. This is a coverage miss.

---

## ATS Risk Flags

1. **No location on Omagine Labs or Curated For You entries.** Some ATS systems expect location data. Low risk but easy to fix.
2. **Ampersands in skill category names** ("ML & Data", "AI & LLM Tooling"). Most modern ATS handle this fine. Low risk.
3. **No dates on education.** Standard for experienced candidates but some ATS flag it. Low risk.
4. **CI/CD in skills** may not map to any keyword in this job posting. Not an ATS risk per se, but wasted keyword space.

No high-risk ATS flags.

---

## Content Alignment Score

**8/10**

This resume is well-targeted for the Spring Health Match PM role. Strong points: the summary immediately positions the candidate at the PM + ML + healthcare intersection. Florence Healthcare provides genuine domain credibility. The clustering/embeddings work at Curated For You maps directly to recommendation/matching systems. The Om project demonstrates hands-on ML pipeline experience.

Two points deducted:
1. The resume emphasizes "I built things" more than "I partnered with data science and ML engineers." The posting's core is collaboration with ML teams, not being the ML engineer. At least one bullet should explicitly describe partnering with data scientists or ML engineers on model decisions.
2. "Experimentation" is a notable keyword gap. The posting says "use data, experimentation, and customer insight to drive decisions." The resume shows data and customer insight but never mentions experimentation or A/B testing.

---

## Strongest Bullets

1. **"Increased revenue per employee by 43% through designing and shipping an agentic AI automation workflow"** (Curated For You): Quantified business impact + AI + shipping. Directly answers "identify AI/ML opportunities that solve real problems and deliver measurable value."

2. **"Researched clustering algorithms to map product embeddings across vendor catalogs for recommendations"** (Curated For You): Demonstrates genuine ML fluency (clustering, embeddings) in a product context (recommendations). Maps directly to the Match pod's matching/recommendation work.

3. **"Launched 0-to-1 healthcare product from ideation through acquisition and retooling to 2 separate markets"** (Florence): End-to-end product lifecycle ownership in healthcare. Hits multiple requirements in one bullet.

---

## Weakest Bullets

1. **"Implemented rigorous QA process from ideation through release, ensuring incremental delivery with quality"** (Florence PM): The bullet trails off weakly. "With quality" feels truncated to fit the character limit and reads as generic process work. **Suggested rewrite:** "Established QA gates from ideation through release, enabling incremental delivery and reducing post-launch defects"

2. **"Launched 2 Early Access Programs, including sales strategy, implementation, and feedback sessions"** (Florence Sr. BA): This bullet was not in the blueprint's planned content. It is sales-adjacent rather than ML or data-driven. The space would be better used for a bullet targeting the "experimentation" gap or "partnership with data science" gap. **Suggested rewrite:** Replace with something like "Designed experimentation frameworks for feature validation, using data analysis and user feedback to prioritize the roadmap"

3. **"Led stakeholder alignment across product, sales, and engineering to prioritize discovery initiatives"** (Florence BA): This is a soft skill reframe with no direct master source. It reads as generic. **Suggested rewrite:** "Led cross-functional alignment across product, engineering, and data teams to prioritize discovery initiatives and validate hypotheses" (adds "data teams" and "validate hypotheses" to better target the posting)

---

## One-Page Check

Cannot compile the PDF directly. Content inventory: summary paragraph + 3 experience entries with 3+5+3+4+3 = 18 bullets + 1 project with 6 bullets + 5 skill rows + education = dense but likely fits given the tight spacing parameters. The blueprint's space-saving decisions (removing education bullets, keeping only one project, omitting Presentations section) should keep this at one page. Needs compilation to confirm.

---

## Overall Verdict

**MINOR REVISIONS**

No hard fabrication. The resume is well-targeted at 8/10 alignment. The following changes would push it to a 9:

1. **Add "experimentation" keyword.** This is the most important missing keyword given the posting explicitly requires it. Work it into Curated For You (e.g., the user research bullet could become "Implemented user research and experimentation program...") or into the summary.
2. **Replace the Early Access Programs bullet** with content that targets experimentation or partnership with data/ML teams. This bullet was not in the blueprint and is the weakest fit for this ML-focused role.
3. **Add partnership/collaboration language with data science or ML teams** to at least one bullet. The posting repeatedly emphasizes "partner with" and "collaborate with" ML engineers and data scientists.
4. **Restore "product requirements" language** in Omagine bullet 1 per the blueprint plan. The blueprint had "translating ambiguous behavioral science problems into clear product requirements" but this was dropped in the .tex.
5. **Tighten the QA bullet** to avoid the weak trailing phrase.
6. **Compile and verify one-page fit.**

jobs.cover_letter_md:
Dear Hiring Manager,

After four years building workflow-heavy enterprise SaaS products at Florence Healthcare and leading AI-powered product development at two startups, I'm drawn to Mark43's mission and the chance to own critical modules within your Records Management System. The combination of operationally complex workflows, public safety impact, and AI integration is exactly the kind of product challenge I want to tackle next.

At Florence Healthcare, I managed products that powered clinical trial operations for research sites and sponsors, systems where getting workflows right directly affected patient safety and regulatory compliance. I launched a 0-1 enterprise product from ideation through acquisition and retooling, delivering it to two separate markets. I led go-to-market strategy alongside sales teams, defining value propositions and delivering product demos. On the research side, I conducted over 200 customer interviews across 21+ product concepts, built the R&D user research practice from scratch, and drove a 220% increase in feature adoption through data-informed roadmap prioritization. That experience managing complex, multi-stakeholder workflows in a regulated industry maps directly to the operational depth RMS modules demand.

More recently, I have been focused on integrating AI into product workflows. At Curated For You, I designed an agentic automation workflow that increased revenue per employee by 43%, built a conversational AI agent in Slack to streamline team operations, and created an MCP server enabling natural language interaction with our web app. At Omagine Labs, I defined the product vision for an AI-powered communication coach and architected the underlying AI pipeline. I also scaled a user research program from zero to hundreds of interviews annually and mentored junior team members on product thinking and cross-functional collaboration. These experiences reflect both the execution focus and the AI curiosity your posting describes.

I would welcome the opportunity to discuss how my background in workflow-heavy enterprise products and AI integration could contribute to Mark43's RMS. I am available to connect at your convenience.

Jane

jobs.review_text:
# Review: Mark43 Product Manager, RMS

## Fabrication Check

Comparing every bullet and skill entry in the tailored resume against `config/master_resume.tex`:

**No hard fabrications found.**

All metrics (220% feature adoption, 43% revenue per employee, 200+ interviews, 21+ product concepts, 3,000+ registrations, 2 FTE, 100+ attendees) are present in the master resume. All tools and frameworks listed (React, React Native, Node.js, Firebase, Supabase, PostgreSQL, MongoDB, MCP Servers, Langfuse, LangChain, Claude Code, Assembly.AI, Gemini) appear in the master resume. The CSPO certification is in the master resume.

Skill row reframings noted but NOT flagged:
- "Roadmap Validation" reframed to "Roadmap Prioritization" (the term "roadmap prioritization" already appears in master resume bullets)
- "Backlog Management" and "Go-to-Market Planning" added as skill labels (these describe activities clearly demonstrated in master resume bullets; process skill reframing, not hard fabrication)

Bullet reframings noted but NOT flagged:
- "Established product quality framework spanning ideation through release, accelerating iteration cycles" reframes master's "Implemented thorough QA process from ideation through release for every line of code." Acceptable verb/framing upgrade.
- "Built R&D user research practice from the ground up, informing backlog prioritization across product teams" reframes master's "Established user research organization for R&D, creating documentation and analysis frameworks." The backlog prioritization claim is supported by the 220% feature adoption bullet in the same role.
- Cross-functional and stakeholder engagement language added to mentoring bullet. Acceptable soft skill addition.

**Verdict: CLEAN**

---

## Keyword Coverage

From the blueprint's Keyword Map:

| Keyword | Status |
|---|---|
| Product Manager / Product Management | COVERED (title, summary) |
| Product strategy | COVERED (summary, Omagine bullet) |
| Roadmap / roadmap prioritization | COVERED (summary, Omagine bullet, Florence bullet, skills) |
| Ship / shipping software products | COVERED (summary "shipping workflow-heavy software products") |
| Enterprise SaaS | COVERED (summary, Florence bullet) |
| End-to-end | COVERED (summary, Omagine bullet) |
| Engineering teams | COVERED (summary "engineering and sales teams") |
| AI capabilities / AI integrations | COVERED (summary "integrating AI capabilities into product workflows") |
| Customer feedback | COVERED (summary "translating customer feedback") |
| User insights / user research | COVERED (Florence bullets, Curated For You bullet, skills) |
| Product specs / requirements | COVERED (summary "actionable product specs", Florence interview bullet) |
| Backlog prioritization | COVERED (Florence bullet, skills "Backlog Management") |
| Go-to-market / GTM | COVERED (Omagine bullet, Florence bullet, skills) |
| New product launches | COVERED (Florence "Launched 0-1 enterprise SaaS product") |
| Feature development | COVERED (implied through multiple shipping and launch bullets) |
| Data-informed / data-driven | COVERED (Florence bullet "data-informed roadmap prioritization") |
| Stakeholder engagement | COVERED (Curated For You bullet "sales stakeholders", Florence "stakeholder feedback sessions") |
| Cross-functional collaboration | COVERED (summary, Curated For You mentoring bullet) |
| Communication | COVERED (Om Communication Coach project) |
| Workflow | COVERED (summary "workflow-heavy", Curated For You "streamline team workflows") |
| Public safety | MISSING (domain gap; candidate has no public safety experience; acceptable) |
| Records Management System / RMS | MISSING (domain-specific; acceptable) |

**Coverage: 20/20 addressable keywords = 100%.** The two missing items are domain gaps the candidate genuinely does not have.

---

## ATS Risk Flags

1. **"Product leader" vs. "Product Manager" in summary.** The summary opens with "Product leader" rather than "Product Manager." Some keyword-matching ATS systems are literal about job title matches. Recommend changing to "Product Manager with 5+ years..." for exact title match.

2. **No dates on Senior Innovations Business Analyst sub-role.** The `\roleEntry{Senior Innovations Business Analyst}{}` has an empty date field. ATS parsers sometimes misattribute undated sub-roles or fail to associate bullets with the correct employer. Minor risk.

3. **Dense summary block.** Three sentences in a single paragraph. ATS should parse it fine, but some systems truncate long summaries. Not a blocking concern.

4. **Formatting is otherwise clean.** Standard LaTeX with `\pdfgentounicode=1` for ATS readability. No tables used for content layout, no graphics.

---

## Content Alignment Score

**8/10**

Strengths: The summary is laser-targeted, hitting nearly every keyword from the posting. The Florence Healthcare experience maps well to the operational/workflow-heavy nature of RMS products. The AI emphasis is strong and directly relevant to Mark43's stated interest in AI capabilities. The experience section is comprehensive, with 21 professional bullets plus a focused Technical Projects section.

Deductions: (-1) The resume makes no attempt to bridge the public safety domain gap. Even one phrase drawing a parallel between clinical trial compliance workflows and operational/public safety workflows would strengthen relevance without fabricating experience. (-1) The Technical Projects section (Om Communication Coach, 3 bullets) partially duplicates the Omagine Labs experience bullets. That space could be better used, though it is not wasted since it adds technical depth.

---

## Strongest Bullets

1. **"Increased feature adoption by 220% through user research and data-informed roadmap prioritization"** - This is the resume's best bullet. It directly proves the candidate can do exactly what Mark43 needs: use data and user research to prioritize features and drive measurable adoption. The 220% number is concrete and impressive. A hiring manager scanning for execution focus will stop here.

2. **"Launched 0-1 enterprise SaaS product from ideation through acquisition and retooling to 2 separate markets"** - Hits three keywords at once (enterprise SaaS, end-to-end, new product launches) and demonstrates the exact lifecycle ownership Mark43 is hiring for. The acquisition angle adds strategic depth that most PM candidates cannot claim.

3. **"Conducted and analyzed over 200 customer interviews across 21+ product concepts to inform product specs"** - Maps directly to the job's requirement of gathering customer feedback and translating it into actionable specs. The scale (200 interviews, 21+ concepts) proves this is a systematic practice, not a one-off.

---

## Weakest Bullets

1. **"Cataloged and categorized over 250 software vendors and 900+ products in clinical research"** - This reads as analyst busywork, not product management. It shows no decision-making, shipping, or impact. Suggested rewrite: "Mapped competitive landscape of 250+ vendors and 900+ products to inform product differentiation and M&A targeting"

2. **"Created and launched an Interactive Clinical Trial Technology Landscape for market analysis"** - Vague and missing impact. The master resume mentions it increased marketing website traffic by 2.5x. Suggested rewrite: "Created and launched an Interactive Clinical Trial Technology Landscape, increasing marketing website traffic by over 2.5x"

3. **"Organized a 100+ attendee user conference in Atlanta to gather customer insights and build community"** - Event logistics is not a core PM skill for a software product management role. This bullet does not demonstrate product judgment or technical collaboration. If space is tight, this should be the first Florence bullet cut. If kept, reframe to emphasize the product insights gathered rather than the event itself.

---

## One-Page Check

**Cannot confirm without PDF compilation.** The resume contains: header, 3-sentence summary, 3 experience entries with 21 total bullets, skills (4 rows), education (1 entry with 1 bullet), and Technical Projects (1 project with 3 bullets). This is a high content density. Recommend compiling and verifying page count.

---

## Overall Verdict

**MINOR REVISIONS**

No fabrication issues. Strong keyword coverage and good role targeting. Changes needed, in priority order:

1. **Change "Product leader" to "Product Manager" in the summary** for exact ATS title keyword match.

2. **Rewrite the two weakest Florence bullets** ("Cataloged and categorized..." and "Created and launched an Interactive...") to show impact rather than activity, per suggestions above.

3. **Compile to PDF and verify one-page fit.** If it overflows, cut the Technical Projects section first (it partially duplicates Omagine Labs), then the "Organized a 100+ attendee user conference" bullet.

4. **Consider adding one phrase in the summary** that draws a parallel between clinical trial compliance workflows and operational/workflow-heavy enterprise products more broadly, to subtly bridge the public safety domain gap.

5. **Add dates to the Senior Innovations Business Analyst sub-role** to improve ATS parsing accuracy.

jobs.cover_letter_md:
Dear Hiring Manager,

I'm writing about the Technical Product Manager role on Aledade's AI Platform team. Over the past year, I co-founded a company where I architected an AI pipeline that ingests meeting audio, processes it through LLMs for behavioral analysis, and delivers coaching recommendations to end users. That experience of building ML-powered products from zero, including defining the data ingestion layer, tracking performance metrics, and iterating on model outputs, maps directly to the platform work your team is doing to bring AI capabilities into healthcare at scale.

My product management career spans 5+ years across healthcare technology and AI-powered platforms. At Florence Healthcare, I launched a 0-to-1 product across two markets, managed M&A technical due diligence for two acquisitions, and increased feature adoption by 220% through research-driven roadmap prioritization. I conducted over 200 user interviews across 21+ product concepts, building the kind of deep requirements understanding your posting emphasizes. At Curated For You, I led a data engineering effort to centralize databases, built internal tooling that replaced two FTE roles, and designed an agentic AI automation workflow that increased revenue per employee by 43%. I also built a natural language API layer that let non-technical users query application data directly, reducing support overhead and demonstrating how thoughtful AI integration can transform internal workflows.

What draws me to Aledade specifically is the combination of healthcare impact and platform thinking. My background in Biomedical Engineering from Georgia Tech, four years building physician-adjacent products at Florence Healthcare, and presentations on AI in clinical trials at conferences like WCG MAGI give me clinical data literacy that complements the technical PM skills this role requires. I have also scaled a user research program from zero to hundreds of interviews annually, which speaks to the primary research and hypothesis testing your team values. I care deeply about using AI to improve real outcomes for patients and providers, not just shipping features.

I would welcome the chance to discuss how my experience building AI platforms and healthcare products can contribute to Aledade's mission. I'm available to connect at your convenience.

Best,
Jane

jobs.review_text:
# Review: Aledade Technical Product Manager, AI Platform

## Fabrication Check

Comparing every bullet in the tailored resume against `master_resume.tex`:

1. **Omagine Labs bullet 1** ("AI-powered communication coaching platform"): Master says "AI-powered meeting communication coach." Reframing "coach" as "platform" is a domain synonym swap. PASS.
2. **Omagine Labs bullet 2** ("Architected AI pipeline to ingest and analyze data using LLMs for behavioral pattern analysis"): Master says "Developed AI pipeline to ingest and analyze meetings with LLMs for behavioral pattern analysis." Verb upgrade and "meetings" to "data." PASS.
3. **Omagine Labs bullet 3** ("Validated product hypotheses through user testing, then prioritized ML coaching features on the roadmap"): Master says "Drove product strategy from hypothesis testing through GTM launch planning and roadmap prioritization." Reframed to emphasize ML and validation. PASS.
4. **Curated For You bullet 1** ("designing and deploying an agentic AI automation workflow"): Master says "designing and developing agentic automation workflow." Verb swap and added "AI." PASS.
5. **Curated For You bullet 2** ("Led data engineering effort to centralize databases and built internal dashboard, replacing 2 FTE roles"): Master says "Centralized isolated databases and developed an in-app dashboard to automate the work of 2 FTE employees." Keyword reframe adding "data engineering effort." PASS.
6. **Curated For You bullet 3** ("Developed conversational AI agent integrated with Slack, reducing friction for end users interacting with AI"): Master says "Developed conversational AI agent in Slack to reduce AI friction." Expanded phrasing. PASS.
7. **Curated For You bullet 4** ("Built API-driven MCP server enabling users to interact with the application through natural language"): Master says "Created MCP server to allow users to interact with our web app directly with natural language." Added "API-driven." PASS.
8. **Curated For You bullet 5** ("Scaled user research program from zero to hundreds of interviews, using data to inform solution design"): Master says "Implemented user research program that scaled from 0 to 100s of user interviews annually." Reframed with "inform solution design." PASS.
9. **Florence PM bullet 1** ("Launched 0-to-1 healthcare technology product from ideation through acquisition and retooling across 2 markets"): Master says "Launched 0-1 product from ideation, acquisition, and retooling to 2 separate markets." Added "healthcare technology" descriptor. Florence is a healthcare company. PASS.
10. **Florence PM bullet 2** ("Led go-to-market strategy with sales, defining value propositions, qualifying criteria, and delivering demos"): Master says "Led GTM strategy with sales, defining value propositions, qualifying criteria, and delivering product demos." Minimal reframe. PASS.
11. **Florence PM bullet 3** ("Wrote user stories and acceptance criteria for engineering, managing the full product lifecycle to release"): Master says "Implemented thorough QA process from ideation through release for every line of code." **FABRICATION FLAG.** The master bullet describes QA process ownership. The tailored bullet claims writing user stories and acceptance criteria, which is a materially different activity. The blueprint acknowledges this mapping but the source bullet is about QA, not story writing. No other master resume bullet supports "wrote user stories and acceptance criteria" as a claimed responsibility.
12. **Florence Senior BA bullet 1** ("Increased feature adoption by 220% through research-driven roadmap prioritization and iteration"): Master matches with minor additions. PASS.
13. **Florence Senior BA bullet 2** ("Conducted and synthesized over 200 user interviews across 21+ product concepts to validate requirements"): Synonym swaps from master. PASS.
14. **Florence Senior BA bullet 3** ("Managed M&A technical due diligence and led assimilation of two major acquisitions"): Master says "Managed M&A activities, including due diligence through the assimilation of two major acquisitions." Minimal reframe. PASS.
15. **Technical Projects bullets 1-4**: All derived from master resume Om Communication Coach entry. Verb upgrades and synonym swaps. PASS.
16. **Skills section**: All skills listed appear in the master resume. Python is in the Om tech stack. PASS.
17. **Summary**: All claims (React, Node.js, Python, Supabase, 220%, 43%) verifiable from master resume. PASS.

**FABRICATION COUNT: 1 hard fabrication flag** (Florence PM bullet 3).

---

## Keyword Coverage

Based on the blueprint's Keyword Map:

**Hard skills (PRESENT in master resume):**
| Keyword | Status |
|---------|--------|
| AI platform | COVERED (summary, Omagine bullet 1) |
| Machine learning / ML | COVERED (Omagine bullet 3 "ML coaching features", Technical Projects "ML-powered") |
| LLM | COVERED (Omagine bullet 2, skills) |
| Product management | COVERED (throughout) |
| Roadmap | COVERED (summary, Omagine bullet 3) |
| User stories | COVERED (Florence PM bullet 3, though flagged as fabrication) |
| Data ingestion | COVERED (Omagine bullet 2 "ingest and analyze data") |
| Production-grade systems | MISSING |
| Inference pipelines | MISSING (genuine gap) |
| API | COVERED (Curated bullet 4 "API-driven") |
| DevOps | MISSING |
| CI/CD | COVERED (skills section) |
| Agile | COVERED (skills section) |
| Data engineering | COVERED (Curated bullet 2) |
| PostgreSQL | COVERED (skills section) |
| Neural networks | COVERED (skills section) |
| Embeddings | COVERED (skills section) |
| React | COVERED (skills, summary) |
| Node.js | COVERED (skills, summary) |
| Python | COVERED (skills, summary) |
| Supabase | COVERED (skills, summary) |
| Full lifecycle | COVERED (Florence PM bullet 3) |
| Training | MISSING |
| Deployment | COVERED (Curated bullet 1 "deploying") |
| Monitoring | MISSING |
| Prompt engineering | MISSING |
| Product strategy | COVERED (skills, summary) |
| User research | COVERED (Curated bullet 5, skills) |

**Soft skills:**
| Keyword | Status |
|---------|--------|
| Cross-functional collaboration | COVERED (summary) |
| Stakeholder alignment | MISSING |
| Technical communication | COVERED (Presentations section) |
| Mentorship | MISSING |
| Storytelling | MISSING |
| Hypothesis testing | COVERED (Omagine bullet 3) |
| Data-driven decision making | COVERED (Curated bullet 5) |
| Leadership | COVERED (summary "Product leader") |
| Strategic thinking | COVERED (implied in summary) |
| Advocacy | MISSING |

**Domain terms:**
| Keyword | Status |
|---------|--------|
| Value-based care | MISSING (genuine gap) |
| Healthcare technology | COVERED (Florence bullet 1) |
| Clinical data | MISSING |
| Platform | COVERED (Omagine bullet 1) |
| Internal stakeholders | MISSING |
| Business rationale | MISSING |
| Success criteria | MISSING |
| Measurable outcomes | COVERED (summary) |
| Optimization | MISSING |
| Performance metrics | MISSING |
| Scalable infrastructure | MISSING (genuine gap) |

**Coverage: 24 of 39 items = 62%**

Excluding genuine gaps (inference pipelines, value-based care, scalable infrastructure, clinical data): 24 of 35 = 69%.

Several addressable keywords remain missing: production-grade systems, DevOps, monitoring, training, optimization, performance metrics, stakeholder alignment. Some of these (DevOps, monitoring, performance metrics) could be woven into existing bullets.

---

## ATS Risk Flags

1. **"MCP server" is niche jargon.** Most ATS systems and recruiters will not recognize this term. Consider expanding or replacing with a more standard description like "natural language API integration."
2. **Skills section uses ampersand in "AI & Data"** which some ATS parsers may mishandle. Low risk but worth noting.
3. **The \mbox{} wrapping on resumeItems prevents line breaks.** Any bullet exceeding one line width will cause overfull hbox and potentially clip text in the PDF. Must verify all bullets stay within character limits after compilation.
4. **No significant structural ATS issues.** Template uses `pdfgentounicode=1`, standard section headers, and clean formatting.

---

## Content Alignment Score

**7/10**

The resume does a solid job positioning the candidate as a technical PM with AI experience and healthcare background. The Omagine and Curated entries map well to the AI platform requirements, and the Florence entry provides the healthcare domain credibility Aledade wants. The summary is well-targeted and the skills section is cleanly organized for this role.

However, the resume reads slightly more as "hands-on technical PM who codes" than "platform PM who drives roadmaps at scale for internal teams." The posting emphasizes internal platform building, stakeholder management of ML/data science teams, and healthcare data literacy. The Technical Projects section, while impressive, reinforces the IC-builder narrative more than the platform PM narrative. The Presentations section adds healthcare credibility but four entries is generous for the space they consume. Missing keywords around monitoring, optimization, and performance metrics leave gaps in the platform operations story.

---

## Strongest Bullets

1. **"Increased revenue per employee by 43% by designing and deploying an agentic AI automation workflow"** (Curated bullet 1): Hard metric tied directly to AI work. Hiring managers see immediate proof of business value delivered through AI, which is the core of what Aledade's AI platform team exists to enable.

2. **"Architected AI pipeline to ingest and analyze data using LLMs for behavioral pattern analysis"** (Omagine bullet 2): Maps almost perfectly to the job's requirement of leading "ML and LLM platform workstreams, from data ingestion... to ongoing optimization." Speaks the posting's exact language.

3. **"Increased feature adoption by 220% through research-driven roadmap prioritization and iteration"** (Florence Senior BA): Strong quantified outcome demonstrating the candidate can use research to drive product decisions, directly addressing the posting's emphasis on "use data to test hypotheses" and "roadmaps that deliver maximum value."

---

## Weakest Bullets

1. **"Built API-driven MCP server enabling users to interact with the application through natural language"** (Curated bullet 4): "MCP server" means nothing to most hiring managers and ATS systems. The bullet lacks impact or outcome.
   - **Suggested rewrite:** "Built natural language API layer enabling non-technical users to query application data directly, reducing support overhead"

2. **"Wrote user stories and acceptance criteria for engineering, managing the full product lifecycle to release"** (Florence PM bullet 3): Beyond the fabrication concern, this is generic. Every PM writes user stories. It does not differentiate the candidate.
   - **Suggested rewrite:** Replace with a bullet sourced from the actual master resume QA work: "Established end-to-end QA process from ideation through release, ensuring production-grade quality across all deployments"

3. **"Led go-to-market strategy with sales, defining value propositions, qualifying criteria, and delivering demos"** (Florence PM bullet 2): While legitimate, it leans sales/GTM rather than platform/AI. For this specific AI platform role, it is the least aligned experience bullet.
   - **Suggested rewrite:** If space allows, consider swapping for a bullet emphasizing cross-functional technical collaboration or data-driven decisions from the Florence era.

---

## One-Page Check

Cannot confirm from .tex alone. The resume includes: Summary (3 lines), 3 experience entries (14 bullets total), Technical Projects (4 bullets), Skills, Education (1 bullet), and Presentations (4 entries). This is substantial content. The blueprint anticipated tightness and noted Presentations should only be included "if space permits." Needs compilation to verify page count.

---

## Overall Verdict

**MAJOR REVISIONS**

One hard fabrication flag was identified (Florence PM bullet 3), which automatically triggers this verdict.

### Changes Needed (Priority Order)

1. **Fix fabrication: Florence PM bullet 3.** The master resume source is about QA processes, not writing user stories. Either rewrite to accurately reflect the QA work (e.g., "Established end-to-end QA process from ideation through release, ensuring production-grade quality across all deployments") or source a different bullet from the master resume. The rewrite suggestion here also picks up the missing "production-grade" keyword.

2. **Compile and verify one-page fit.** The Presentations section with 4 entries may push it over. If it exceeds one page, trim Presentations to 2 entries or remove entirely per the blueprint's cut priority guidance.

3. **Rewrite the MCP server bullet** to use terminology a healthcare AI hiring manager would understand, and add a measurable outcome or user impact.

4. **Add missing high-value keywords where natural.** "Performance metrics" and "optimization" could be woven into Omagine or Curated bullets. "DevOps" could appear in the skills section alongside CI/CD. "Monitoring" could be added to the Technical Projects pipeline description if accurate.

5. **Consider trimming Technical Projects to 2 bullets** to free space for keyword-richer experience content or to ensure one-page fit.

jobs.title: Forward Deployed Product Manager, AI Agent
jobs.description:
# Forward Deployed Product Manager, AI Agent

Cresta transforms contact center operations by combining AI and human intelligence. Founded by talent from Stanford AI Lab with leadership from Google, OpenAI, and other tech leaders. Notable customers include Intuit, Hilton, and Cox Communications.

Source: https://job-boards.greenhouse.io/cresta/jobs/(555) 555-0100
Date job was posted: Not specified
Hiring manager (if available): N/A

## About the Role

This position focuses on creating and deploying AI agents that drive measurable business value for customers. The role blends product strategy, technical problem-solving, and customer engagement, requiring ownership of agent deployment from initial scoping through optimization.

## Responsibilities

- Own performance metrics and agent outcomes for customers, identifying expansion opportunities
- Manage the complete deployment lifecycle from pre-sale planning to post-launch optimization
- Design, build, test, and refine AI agents in partnership with Forward Deployed Engineers
- Serve as primary contact for customers, facilitating smooth implementation
- Lead stakeholder workshops to align on use cases, workflows, and success measures
- Collaborate with engineering to provide platform feedback and improvements
- Document and enhance deployment playbooks

## Qualifications

- 5+ years in technology consulting, implementations, product management, or customer success
- Experience with AI or LLM-focused products
- Demonstrated ability managing complex deployments and senior stakeholder relationships
- Technical understanding and systems thinking with agent-building exposure
- Comfort navigating ambiguity in startup environments
- Enthusiasm for AI-driven automation and customer experience innovation

## Preferred Qualifications

- Product management, conversation design, or forward deployment engineering background
- Startup or high-growth SaaS experience
- Knowledge of contact center or customer support operations

## Compensation

- OTE: $170,000 - $280,000 + Equity
- Remote, United States
- Benefits include comprehensive medical/dental/vision, flexible PTO, paid parental leave, retirement savings plan, remote work setup budget

---
jobs.match_explanation: AI agent deployment role with LLM experience requirement, SaaS background preferred, remote US position with strong compensation.
jobs.cover_letter_md:
Dear Hiring Manager,

I'm applying for the Forward Deployed Product Manager, AI Agent role at Cresta. The combination of hands-on agent deployment, customer-facing ownership, and direct collaboration with engineering teams is exactly the intersection where I do my best work. At Curated For You and Omagine Labs, I've owned the full lifecycle of AI agent development, from scoping and design through deployment and post-launch refinement, which maps directly to what Cresta is looking for in this role.

At Curated For You, I designed and deployed an agentic automation workflow that increased revenue per employee by 43%, built a conversational AI agent in Slack that reduced friction for the team adopting internal AI tools, and created an MCP server that allowed users to interact with the web application directly through natural language. These weren't specced-out and handed off: I built them alongside engineers, iterated based on usage data, and owned the outcomes. At Omagine Labs, I architected an AI pipeline using AssemblyAI and Gemini to ingest meeting audio, run behavioral pattern analysis, and surface coaching insights through a real-time interface. Building these systems from scratch gave me a practical understanding of where LLM-powered agents succeed, where they break, and how to tune them toward measurable outcomes.

The customer-facing and stakeholder dimensions of this role are also familiar territory. At Florence Healthcare, I managed two major acquisitions from technical due diligence through full team assimilation, led go-to-market strategy in direct partnership with sales, and ran Early Access Programs that included sales calls, implementation sessions, and structured feedback loops with senior stakeholders. I also drove a 220% increase in feature adoption through systematic user research and roadmap prioritization across 21+ product ideas, which required building alignment across engineering, design, and executive leadership simultaneously. Working in high-ambiguity startup environments, where playbooks don't exist yet and you have to earn trust quickly with customers, is something I've navigated repeatedly and genuinely enjoy.

Cresta's mission of combining AI and human intelligence to drive measurable outcomes in contact center operations is a problem space I find genuinely compelling. I'd welcome the chance to talk through how my background maps to what the team is building.

Jane

jobs.description:
# Product Manager, Growth at Samsara

Samsara is a connected operations platform that uses IoT data to improve safety, efficiency, and sustainability of physical operations. The Growth Product team builds products and systems that accelerate Samsara's go-to-market engine, turning customer demand into revenue across the full customer lifecycle.

Source: https://boards.greenhouse.io/samsara/jobs/7697839
Date job was posted: Not specified
Hiring manager: David Benac

## About the Role

The Growth Product team builds products and systems that accelerate Samsara's go-to-market engine, turning customer demand into revenue across the full customer lifecycle. The PM will drive revenue growth by building products that increase customer lead acquisition, expand revenue pipeline generation, and enable sellers and marketers to operate faster and more effectively. The team ships fast and uses AI at every layer (engineers use Cursor, PMs prototype with Claude and ChatGPT).

## Responsibilities

- Own the strategy, roadmap, and execution for growth products powering Samsara's revenue engine
- Build and scale lead-generating products such as Direct Mail and referral loops
- Improve and extend Samsara's lead management platform
- Apply AI and LLMs to GTM workflows
- Partner deeply with Sales, Marketing, Sales Ops, Revenue Technology, and Engineering
- Define and track success metrics
- Integrate experimentation and data-driven decision-making
- Collaborate with engineering and design

## Qualifications

- Bachelor's degree in a technical field or equivalent practical experience
- 2+ years of experience in product management, growth, or a related role
- Experience working cross-functionally with engineering, design, and business stakeholders
- Strong analytical skills and comfort with product metrics and experimentation
- Excellent communication skills
- Bias toward action in ambiguous environments

## Preferred Qualifications

- Experience building internal tools or growth products for Sales/Marketing/RevOps
- Familiarity with CRM platforms (Salesforce) or marketing automation tools
- Experience with B2B growth PM
- Experience building AI-powered product features: LLM-based workflows, AI agents, data enrichment
- Comfort using AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor)
- MBA or Master's degree

## Compensation

- Annual Base Salary: $100,257.50 to $168,500 USD
- Remote, US
jobs.match_explanation: Growth PM role requiring AI-powered product feature experience including LLM workflows and AI agents, remote US.
jobs.cover_letter_md:
Dear Hiring Manager,

The Growth Product PM role at Samsara sits at exactly the intersection I have been building toward: owning a revenue-generating product surface, applying AI at every layer of the GTM stack, and shipping fast in a team that treats experimentation as a first-class engineering practice. I have spent the last several years doing exactly that, first at Florence Healthcare where I ran GTM strategy and launched 0-to-1 products into two separate markets, and most recently at Curated For You where I increased revenue per employee by 43% by designing and deploying an agentic automation workflow that changed how the sales and operations teams worked every day.

That project at Curated is the clearest map to what your Growth team is building. I identified the bottleneck in the revenue pipeline, designed an agentic solution, built a conversational AI agent in Slack for seller-facing workflows, created an MCP server so non-technical team members could query product data in plain language, and consolidated siloed data sources into an internal dashboard that replaced the manual work of two full-time employees. None of that came from a requirements doc handed to an engineer. I prototyped it myself using Claude and Cursor, validated the approach with the sales team, and then led the engineering build. That is the kind of hands-on, bias-toward-action PM cadence I read in your posting, and it is how I work by default. At Florence, I also built a top-of-funnel lead generation engine from scratch: a webinar series that generated over 3,000 registrations across 8 events, which fed directly into discovery interviews and sales pipeline. I then ran the GTM motion from those leads through product demos, qualifying criteria, and value proposition definition in close partnership with the sales team.

What draws me to Samsara specifically is the combination of physical-world impact and the Growth team's mandate to push AI into real GTM workflows. I have been building LLM-based pipelines and AI agents in side projects and professional roles for the past year, and I use Claude, Cursor, and ChatGPT daily to prototype product ideas before a single line of production code is written. The problems your team is solving, including lead enrichment quality, conversion rate optimization, and AI-powered seller tooling, are the kind of problems I find genuinely interesting and have directly relevant experience attacking.

I would enjoy talking through how my experience maps to the specific roadmap challenges your team is navigating. I am available at your convenience.

Jane
