jobs.description:
# Product Manager at Daymark Health

Daymark Health is a value-based oncology company focused on transforming cancer care delivery. The platform provides comprehensive patient support including care navigation, symptom management, behavioral health services, and social resources, combined with evidence-based interventions and hybrid care models.

Source: https://job-boards.greenhouse.io/daymarkhealth/jobs/(555) 555-0100
Date job was posted: N/A
Hiring manager (if available): N/A

## About the Role

Product Manager role at Daymark Health, fully remote with preference for candidates in New York, Philadelphia, or Boston. The position focuses on leading discovery and design for enrollment workflows, enhancing the proprietary Care Management platform, and identifying AI automation opportunities.

## Responsibilities

- Lead discovery and design for enrollment workflows to expand patient reach
- Enhance the proprietary Care Management platform for improved care delivery
- Conduct user research through shadowing clinicians, care teams, and patients
- Manage full product lifecycle from discovery through launch and iteration
- Provide hands-on support with reporting, workflow refinement, and problem-solving
- Identify and implement AI automation opportunities

## Qualifications

- 5+ years of product management experience
- Track record building user-centric products with complex workflows
- Healthcare industry experience, preferably in care delivery settings
- Deep understanding of clinician and patient workflows
- Strong written and verbal communication across technical and clinical teams
- Regular AI tool usage and enthusiasm for emerging technologies
- Alignment with mission to improve healthcare

## Preferred Qualifications

- Healthcare care delivery experience preferred

## Compensation

- Salary: $155,000-$175,000 annually (based on location and experience)
- Location: Remote (New York, Philadelphia, or Boston preferred)
- Note: The company does not sponsor work visas for this role.

---
jobs.match_explanation: Healthcare product manager role at a remote-friendly company with AI automation focus, well-aligned with target seniority and domain preferences.
jobs.cover_letter_md:
Dear Hiring Manager,

When I saw Daymark Health's Product Manager role, I immediately recognized the intersection of healthcare product management, enrollment workflows, and AI automation that has defined the last five years of my career. At Florence Healthcare, I spent four years building products for clinical research teams, conducting over 200 user interviews with clinicians and site coordinators, and launching Early Access Programs centered on enrollment workflows and iterative feedback. That experience gave me a deep appreciation for the complexity of care delivery workflows, which is exactly what this role demands.

My healthcare product management experience maps directly to what Daymark needs. At Florence, I launched a 0-to-1 healthcare product from ideation through acquisition and retooling for two separate markets, increased feature adoption by 220% through user research and data-driven roadmap prioritization, and cataloged 250+ vendors and 900+ products to map patient and clinician workflows across the clinical trial landscape. I also presented on topics like "The Enrollment Revolution" and "AI in Clinical Trials" at industry conferences, reflecting my deep engagement with the problems Daymark is solving. More recently, at Curated For You, I designed an agentic AI automation workflow that increased revenue per employee by 43%, and I built a conversational AI agent in Slack to reduce friction and drive team adoption of AI tools.

Beyond the technical fit, I care about the mission. My biomedical engineering degree from Georgia Tech was a deliberate choice to work at the intersection of technology and human health, and every role since has reinforced that direction. I thrive in hands-on environments where I can shadow users, refine workflows, and ship improvements quickly. The combination of user research, care management platform development, and AI automation at Daymark is the kind of work I find most meaningful.

I would welcome the chance to discuss how my healthcare PM background and AI experience can help Daymark expand patient reach and improve care delivery. I am available to connect at your convenience.

Best,
Jane
jobs.application_answers_text:
# Application Answers: Daymark Health, Product Manager

## What's a recent healthcare tech product you've worked on? (50 words or less)

At Florence Healthcare, I researched many new product ideas to determine which ones had the highest ROI and strategic overlap for the business. One of those ideas was a CRM tool specifically for clinical trial sites to manage recruitment of their trials. Due to my research, we acquired a company which I joined as a PM to oversee the assimilation of the team and retool specifically for our markets. I lead roadmap planning, GTM planning and execution with the sales team, implemented the business's QA process for the new team, and led company wide presentations about the tool.
jobs.review_text:
# Review: Daymark Health, Product Manager

## Fabrication Check

Every bullet in the tailored resume was compared against `master_resume.tex`. No hard fabrications found.

- **Omagine Labs:** All three bullets map directly to master resume sources. "Communication coaching platform" reframes "meeting communication coach." "Behavioral patterns, delivering actionable insights" reframes "meetings with LLMs for behavioral pattern analysis." "Go-to-market" expands "GTM." All acceptable keyword reframing.
- **Curated For You:** All five bullets trace to master resume entries. "Deploying" vs. "developing" is a verb swap. "Workflow refinement, automating reporting for 2 FTEs" reframes "automate the work of 2 FTE employees." "Reducing friction and improving team adoption of AI tools" expands "reduce AI friction" with an inferred outcome; acceptable reframe.
- **Florence Healthcare (Product Manager):** "Healthcare product" adds the domain qualifier to "product"; Florence Healthcare is a healthcare company, so this is accurate context. "Leading technical integration and transition to product team" reframes "moved to product team after transition" with a stronger active voice. "Map patient and clinician workflows" reframes the cataloging bullet using job posting language. All acceptable.
- **Florence Healthcare (Sr. IBA):** "Data-driven roadmap prioritization" expands "roadmap prioritization." "Informing discovery and design" added to the user interviews bullet is an acceptable framing addition. "Enrollment workflows" added to the Early Access Programs bullet uses the posting's exact terminology to describe the same work. "Interactive clinical trial technology landscape, increasing website traffic by over 2.5x" combines the landscape creation bullet and the traffic metric from Technical Projects. All traceable.
- **Florence Healthcare (IBA):** Both bullets are verbatim from master resume.
- **Summary metrics:** 200+ interviews, 220% adoption, 43% efficiency all trace to specific master resume bullets.
- **Skills:** "Product Lifecycle Management" is a soft skill reframe, not a tool or platform. All listed tools (Claude Code, MCP Servers, Langfuse, LangChain, React, React Native, Node.js, Firebase, Supabase, PostgreSQL, CI/CD Automation, CSPO) appear in the master resume.

**VERDICT: No hard fabrications detected. CLEAN.**

## Keyword Coverage

| Keyword | Status |
|---|---|
| product management | COVERED (title, summary, multiple bullets) |
| product lifecycle | COVERED (skills: "Product Lifecycle Management") |
| discovery | COVERED (Florence Sr. IBA: "informing discovery and design") |
| enrollment workflows | COVERED (Florence Sr. IBA: "enrollment workflows") |
| user research | COVERED (summary, Curated For You, Florence, skills) |
| care delivery | COVERED (summary: "improve care delivery") |
| clinician workflows | COVERED (Florence PM: "map patient and clinician workflows") |
| patient workflows | COVERED (Florence PM: "map patient and clinician workflows") |
| AI automation | COVERED (Curated For You: "agentic AI automation workflow") |
| care management platform | MISSING |
| user-centric | COVERED (summary: "user-centric products") |
| complex workflows | COVERED (summary: "complex clinical workflows") |
| cross-functional collaboration | COVERED (Curated For You mentoring bullet) |
| communication | COVERED (Omagine: "communication coaching platform") |
| mission-driven | COVERED (summary: "mission-driven product development") |
| problem-solving | COVERED (summary: "hands-on problem-solving") |
| workflow refinement | COVERED (Curated For You: "workflow refinement") |
| reporting | COVERED (Curated For You: "automating reporting") |
| hands-on | COVERED (summary: "hands-on problem-solving") |
| launch | COVERED (multiple bullets) |
| iteration | MISSING |
| emerging technologies | COVERED (summary: "emerging technologies") |

**Coverage: 20/22 = 91%**

**Notes on missing keywords:**
- "care management platform" is the core product the PM would own at Daymark. This is a meaningful gap. It could be woven into the summary (e.g., "launching user-centric care management products") or a Florence bullet.
- "iteration" appears conceptually in "iterative feedback sessions" but the standalone word "iteration" is absent. Minor gap.

## ATS Risk Flags

1. **No structural issues.** Template uses `\pdfgentounicode=1` for proper text extraction, clean section headings, and standard bullet formatting.
2. **Florence sub-roles:** Three role titles under one company (Product Manager, Senior Innovations Business Analyst, Innovations Business Analyst) could confuse some ATS systems about role association. This is a minor, common risk and the standard approach for progressive roles.
3. **Summary length (5 lines):** On the longer side but within acceptable range for modern ATS parsers.

**No critical ATS risks identified.**

## Content Alignment Score

**8/10**

The resume reads as genuinely targeted for this healthcare PM role. The summary directly mirrors Daymark's posting language (care delivery, user-centric products, complex clinical workflows, emerging technologies, mission-driven). Florence Healthcare provides 4 years of domain-relevant experience with concrete proof points: 200+ user interviews, enrollment workflows, clinician/patient workflow mapping, and healthcare product launches. The AI experience across Omagine and Curated For You directly addresses the AI automation requirement.

Why not higher: (1) "Care management platform" is absent, and that is the literal product this PM would own. (2) The Omagine Labs and Curated For You sections, while demonstrating AI skills, have no healthcare connection, which creates a slight disconnect in the narrative. (3) The M&A bullet, while impressive, reads as corporate development rather than care delivery product work.

## Strongest Bullets

1. **"Conducted and analyzed over 200 user interviews across 21+ product ideas, informing discovery and design"** -- This is the standout bullet. The posting explicitly requires conducting user research by shadowing clinicians, care teams, and patients. 200+ interviews is a concrete, impressive number, and "informing discovery and design" mirrors the posting's language exactly.

2. **"Launched 2 Early Access Programs with enrollment workflows, sales strategy, and iterative feedback sessions"** -- Hits two of the posting's most specific requirements simultaneously: enrollment workflows and the full product lifecycle from discovery through launch. Early Access Programs demonstrate comfort with ambiguity and new product introduction, which is exactly what Daymark needs.

3. **"Increased revenue per employee by 43% through designing and deploying an agentic AI automation workflow"** -- Quantified AI impact with a hard metric. The posting asks for identifying and implementing AI automation opportunities. This proves the candidate ships AI with measurable business results, not just experiments with it.

## Weakest Bullets

1. **"Managed assimilation of an acquired product, leading technical integration and transition to product team"** -- This reads as corporate process management, not care delivery product work. A Daymark hiring manager wants to see someone who improves patient outcomes, not someone who manages acquisitions. **Suggested rewrite:** "Led technical integration of acquired healthcare product, unifying clinical workflows to streamline care delivery across platforms" (if accurate to the scope of work).

2. **"Managed M&A activities, including due diligence through the assimilation of two major acquisitions"** -- Same problem. Two M&A bullets in the Florence section is one too many for a care delivery PM role. **Suggested rewrite:** Cut this bullet entirely and replace with a healthcare-focused bullet from the master resume, or reframe to emphasize the product evaluation and workflow integration aspects rather than the M&A mechanics.

3. **"Mentored an intern on user research methodology and product development processes"** -- This is the lowest-impact bullet on the page. It is generic, brief, and does not differentiate the candidate. The mentoring bullet in Curated For You already covers leadership. **Suggested rewrite:** Replace with the webinar series bullet from the master resume ("Created webinar series generating 3,000+ registrations across 8 events") which demonstrates reach and stakeholder engagement, or use the space for a bullet about hands-on clinical workflow problem-solving.

## One-Page Check

**CONFIRMED.** The PDF renders as exactly one page. Content fills the page well with the six Presentations entries anchoring the bottom. No overflow risk.

## Overall Verdict

**MINOR REVISIONS**

No fabrication issues. The resume is well-targeted with strong healthcare PM positioning and good keyword coverage at 91%. Four specific changes needed, in priority order:

1. **Add "care management platform" to the summary or a Florence bullet.** This is the product the PM would own. Example: change "launching user-centric products with complex clinical workflows" to "launching user-centric care management products with complex clinical workflows."
2. **Replace the intern mentoring bullet** (Florence IBA) with something higher-impact. The webinar series bullet (3,000+ registrations) or a bullet emphasizing hands-on clinical workflow problem-solving would better serve this role.
3. **Consolidate M&A references.** Two M&A bullets (PM bullet 3 and Sr. IBA bullet 4) is redundant for a care delivery PM role. Keep one, reframe it to emphasize healthcare product integration and workflow continuity, and replace the other with a more relevant bullet.
4. **Add "iteration" explicitly** to close the last keyword gap. The summary could read: "...and mission-driven iteration on product development" or a similar natural insertion.

jobs.title: Product Manager, Native Experience & Growth
jobs.description:
# Product Manager, Native Experience & Growth

Cohere seeks a PM to oversee user engagement across North, an enterprise AI platform. This role spans three interconnected areas: native applications (iOS, Android, macOS), front-end platform infrastructure, and user lifecycle optimization.

Source: https://www.builtinnyc.com/job/product-manager-native-experience-growth/8718670
Date job was posted: Reposted 6 days ago; valid through May 6, 2026
Hiring manager (if available): N/A

## About the Role

Product Manager role at Cohere focused on native experience and growth for the North enterprise AI platform. Remote or hybrid in San Francisco, New York, or Toronto, with EST hours preferred. Full-time, senior level (5+ years required).

## Responsibilities

- Build and execute roadmaps for mobile apps while balancing native capabilities with cross-platform consistency
- Own shared component systems enabling enterprise customization, white-labeling, and branding
- Design onboarding flows and engagement strategies to reduce time-to-value
- Establish analytics instrumentation and run A/B tests to improve key metrics
- Engage directly with enterprise customers to validate product decisions
- Collaborate with distributed design and engineering teams

## Qualifications

- 5+ years in product management, with significant experience in mobile/native apps, front-end platforms, or growth/lifecycle PM
- Demonstrated success shipping mobile or platform-layer products impacting adoption or retention
- Deep knowledge of iOS HIG and Material Design principles
- Data-driven approach: define metrics upfront, instrument products, run and interpret experiments
- Strong cross-functional collaboration skills
- Execution-focused mindset

## Preferred Qualifications

- Front-end platform experience (design systems, white-labeling, component libraries)
- Enterprise mobile compliance knowledge (MDM/MAM, encryption)
- Familiarity with agentic AI systems or LLMs in mobile/desktop contexts

## Compensation

- No salary disclosed
- Competitive compensation and equity options
- Health, dental, and mental health benefits
- 100% parental leave top-up (6 months)
- 6 weeks vacation
- Wellness and enrichment stipends

---
jobs.match_explanation: AI/LLM-focused product management role at a leading enterprise AI company with remote or NYC hybrid options and growth PM responsibilities.
jobs.cover_letter_md:
Dear Cohere Hiring Team,

I'm applying for the Product Manager, Native Experience & Growth role on North. The combination of native apps, front-end platform work, and lifecycle optimization maps closely to how I've spent the last few years: shipping a native macOS coaching app at Omagine Labs, launching an iOS social app that scaled to 250 users in two months, and driving a 220% increase in feature adoption at Florence Healthcare through research-driven roadmap prioritization.

Across 5+ years in product management, I've consistently owned products end-to-end where adoption and engagement were the scoreboard. At Florence, I launched a 0-to-1 product into two enterprise markets and led GTM with sales, defining value props and running product demos directly with enterprise customers. As Senior Innovations Business Analyst, I conducted and analyzed over 200 user interviews across 21+ product concepts to validate decisions before we built. At Curated For You, I increased revenue per employee by 43% through an agentic automation workflow and built a Slack-native conversational AI agent that lowered the friction of getting users to value, the same time-to-value problem North's onboarding work targets.

The native and AI pieces of this role are where I'm currently spending all my time. At Omagine Labs, I architected an AI pipeline that uses LLMs for behavioral pattern analysis and built the macOS desktop client with React and Electron, capturing system audio and surfacing engagement metrics like speaking time, interruption patterns, and sentiment. Working hands-on with agentic systems and LLMs in a desktop context has shaped how I think about latency, trust, and what "native" should actually feel like when AI is the core experience. I also genuinely enjoy partnering with distributed design and engineering teams; mentorship and tight cross-functional loops have been central to how I operate.

Cohere's enterprise focus and the scope of North, where mobile, platform, and growth all converge under one PM, is exactly the kind of surface area I want to own next. I'd welcome the chance to discuss how I can contribute.

Best,
Jane

# Technical Product Manager at Anara

Anara is an AI platform for scientific advancement, aiming to achieve 100 years of scientific progress in the next 10 years. Within two years, they have grown to millions of users across academic labs and R&D teams, approaching $10M in annual revenue. Backed by Y Combinator and founders of GitHub and Reddit.

Source: https://jobs.ashbyhq.com/anara/f69dcabe-1f30-46ce-8407-ba03f55f2ced
Date job was posted: N/A
Hiring manager (if available): N/A

## About the Role

As the organization's first product manager, you will partner with the founder to direct Anara's evolution and establish how researchers interact with AI tools. The position focuses on creating innovative solutions that reshape scientific discovery methodologies. The 10-person team is seeking founding-stage talent.

## Responsibilities

- Conduct user discovery via interviews, session analysis, and data evaluation
- Convert cutting-edge AI developments into researcher-focused features
- Synthesize customer insights to drive product decisions
- Streamline workflows to minimize friction and churn
- Collaborate with engineering to maintain Anara's competitive advantage

## Qualifications

- 2+ combined years in product management and engineering roles, with minimum 1 year as a professional engineer
- Track record building and scaling products to thousands of users independently
- Experience in high-growth, fast-paced startup environments
- Strong design sensibility and product intuition
- Ability to operate autonomously with minimal direction

## Preferred Qualifications

- N/A (none listed)

## Compensation

- Salary: $150,000-$200,000
- Equity: 0.50%-1.00%
- Premium healthcare (medical, dental)
- Flexible work hours
- San Francisco office with marina views
- Visa sponsorship available
- Location: San Francisco or Remote (North America, Europe)

---
jobs.match_explanation: Inaugural PM role at a YC-backed AI tools startup with remote option, requiring 2+ years experience and strong product intuition.
jobs.cover_letter_md:
Dear Hiring Manager,

Anara's mission of compressing 100 years of scientific progress into the next decade is exactly the kind of audacious bet I want to build for. As a Biomedical Engineering graduate from Georgia Tech who moved into product management and then taught myself to ship full-stack software, I sit at an unusual intersection: I understand what researchers need from tools, I know how to translate that into product decisions, and I can write the code to test hypotheses before asking engineering to commit a sprint. That combination is what the founding Technical PM role requires, and it is what I bring.

At Florence Healthcare, I built the user research organization from scratch, conducted and synthesized 200+ user interviews across 21 product ideas, and grew feature adoption by 220% through data-driven roadmap prioritization. I launched two Early Access Programs, coordinating discovery, implementation, and feedback sessions that validated product-market fit before full release. At Curated For You, I increased revenue per employee by 43% by independently scoping, building, and shipping an agentic automation workflow, and I implemented a user research program that scaled from zero to hundreds of interviews annually. In both roles I operated with minimal direction, owned the full product lifecycle, and partnered directly with engineering. Most recently, I co-founded Omagine Labs, where I am directing 0-1 development of an AI-powered communication coach: architecting the LLM pipeline, owning the roadmap, and writing production code alongside the technical work.

What draws me specifically to Anara is the user base. Researchers are a demanding audience who can spot a shallow tool immediately. My engineering background gives me a head start on speaking their language and uncovering the friction points they will not always articulate. I know how to run structured discovery with technical users, synthesize behavioral data from session analysis, and convert those insights into features that reduce friction and drive retention. I am also comfortable being the first PM at a 10-person team. At Omagine, I stood up product operations from nothing. At Florence, I was often the sole PM voice shaping decisions that affected clinical research teams across dozens of institutions.

I would welcome the chance to talk through how my background maps to what you are building. I am available at your convenience and genuinely excited about this work.

Jane
jobs.application_answers_text:
# Application Answers: Anara — Technical Product Manager

---

## Write a short note on a project you're most proud of. (Provide a link to use, if possible)

The project I'm most proud of is Om Communication Coach, an AI-powered desktop application I designed and built from scratch to analyze meeting audio and coach users on their communication patterns. I own the full stack: a React and Electron frontend that captures system audio, an Assembly.AI transcription pipeline, and a Gemini-powered behavioral analysis layer that extracts metrics like speaking time, interruption frequency, and sentiment across meeting participants. The system surfaces those insights as actionable coaching feedback after each meeting.

What makes this project meaningful to me is that it required me to wear every hat simultaneously. I conducted user discovery to define what coaching signals actually change behavior, architected the AI pipeline, wrote the production code, and iterated on the product based on early user feedback. The work mirrors exactly how I want to operate at Anara: close to the user, technically hands-on, and focused on turning complex AI capabilities into tools that genuinely help people do their jobs better.

I don't have a public link yet, as the product is still in early access, but I'm happy to share a demo recording or walk through the product live during our conversation.

Based in New York City.

## Responsibilities

- Define and launch AI-powered products that transform customer engagement
- Spend time with customers understanding workflows and translating needs into solutions
- Own the product roadmap, balancing technical tradeoffs with customer value
- Communicate complex technical concepts to both engineers and non-technical stakeholders

- Obsessed with earning customer love and delivering customer impact
- Ability to move quickly and handle ambiguous problems decisively

## Preferred Qualifications

- Experience at an early-stage, high-growth startup

jobs.description:
# AI Agent Product Manager

Hello Patient seeks a technical, high-agency product manager to oversee the complete delivery of AI agents in customer environments. The company builds voice, SMS, and chat agents designed to integrate with healthcare practice operations.

Source: https://jobs.heyhealthtech.com/jobs/ai-agent-product-manager-f99fd0ff
Date job was posted: 4 days ago (approx. April 9, 2026)
Hiring manager (if available): N/A

## About the Role

Each customer deployment functions as a product launch with distinct workflows and integration requirements. This role spans from initial scoping through go-live, including ownership of customer-facing roadmaps and requirements definition.

The position blends product ownership with hands-on agent development. You'll gather customer requirements, translate them into agent logic and workflows, and implement behavior using prompts, guardrails, tools, and evaluations.

## Responsibilities

- Lead end-to-end delivery of new AI agent deployments as sole accountable owner
- Translate customer requirements into production-ready agent behavior
- Perform hands-on prompt engineering for conversation flows and decision logic
- Handle real healthcare complexity including scheduling, patient context, and EMR integration
- Improve agent performance through structured evaluations and customer feedback
- Own deployments for new workflows and verticals without established playbooks
- Coordinate across conversational design and customer engineering teams
- Identify patterns and develop playbooks for scaling implementations

## Qualifications

- 3+ years in Product Management, technical program management, or equivalent (software engineer, founder experience acceptable)
- Strong technical product background with API and integration understanding
- Demonstrated ability shipping in ambiguous, fast-moving environments
- Proven cross-functional influence and stakeholder management
- Action-oriented mindset; preference for decisive iteration over prolonged analysis

## Preferred Qualifications

- AI-native product, agent, or LLM platform experience
- Healthcare or legacy systems industry background
- Hands-on bot building and conversational design experience
- Founder or early-stage startup involvement
- Voice AI or real-time orchestration system familiarity
- Portfolio of shipped conversational experiences (IVR, chatbots, SMS flows)
- Agent quality evaluation practice knowledge

## Compensation

- Base Salary: $160,000 - $200,000 USD annually
- Equity: Meaningful ownership stake in growing healthtech startup
- Location: Remote (United States)

---
jobs.match_explanation: Strong match for a mid-level PM role combining AI agent development, healthcare domain, conversational AI, and LLM experience, fully remote in the US.
jobs.cover_letter_md:
Dear Hiring Manager,

I spent the last four years building healthcare products at Florence Healthcare and the last year building conversational AI agents from scratch. The AI Agent Product Manager role at Hello Patient sits exactly at that intersection, and I want to be the person owning end-to-end delivery of your agent deployments.

At Curated For You, I built and deployed a conversational AI agent in Slack, translating customer requirements into production-ready agent behavior. I also created an MCP server that let users interact with our platform using natural language, integrating API and tool orchestration under the hood. That hands-on agent work drove a 43% increase in revenue per employee through agentic automation workflows. In parallel, I co-founded Omagine Labs, where I led 0-to-1 delivery of an AI-powered coaching agent from scoping through go-live, engineering the LLM pipeline with prompt logic and structured evaluations. These are the same muscles your role demands: taking a customer's needs, translating them into agent logic with prompts, guardrails, and evaluations, then iterating until the deployment works in production.

My four years at Florence Healthcare gave me direct experience navigating healthcare complexity. I launched a 0-to-1 product from ideation through acquisition and retooling to two separate markets, led GTM with sales across customer deployments, and worked with EMR integration requirements. I conducted over 200 user interviews across 21+ product concepts, identifying patterns and developing playbooks for scaling implementations. That pattern recognition, knowing which pieces of one customer deployment generalize to the next, is exactly what Hello Patient needs as you scale agent deployments across new workflows and verticals.

I would genuinely enjoy the work of taking a customer's messy scheduling or patient communication workflows and turning them into reliable AI agent behavior. I am available to discuss the role at your convenience.

Jane
jobs.application_answers_text:
# Application Answers: Hello Patient — AI Agent Product Manager

## Which location are you applying for? (Austin, TX - In Person / US - Remote)

US - Remote

## Are you currently based in Austin, TX? (Yes / No)

No

## Are you open to relocating to Austin, TX? (Yes, I am willing to relocate / Possibly — I'd like to learn more about the role first / No, I am not open to relocating / Not applicable — I am already based in Austin)

Possibly — I'd like to learn more about the role first

## What's your experience working hands-on with technical systems (e.g., APIs, integrations, automation)?

I work hands-on with technical systems daily. At Curated For You, I built an MCP server that lets users interact with our web application through natural language, requiring deep integration with our platform's APIs and data layer. I also designed and developed an agentic automation workflow that increased revenue per employee by 43%, connecting multiple systems and automating previously manual processes. On the integration side, I created a conversational AI agent in Slack that required orchestrating LLM calls, prompt logic, and tool integrations to deliver useful responses in a production environment.

At Omagine Labs, I engineered an AI pipeline that ingests meeting audio, runs it through Assembly.AI for transcription, and passes it to Gemini for behavioral analysis. This pipeline coordinates multiple APIs, handles audio processing via FFmpeg, and uses Langfuse for observability. I also built the full React and Node.js frontend and backend, managing Supabase for data persistence.

My technical stack includes React, React Native, Node.js, Firebase, Supabase, PostgreSQL, LangChain, and Langfuse. I am not a PM who hands off specs to engineers. I prototype, build, and ship working software to validate ideas before committing engineering resources.

## Describe something you've personally shipped end-to-end in a fast-moving or ambiguous environment. What was your role, what did you build, and what happened?

At Curated For You, I joined as the first product hire at a small company with no formal product function. Within my first months, I identified that AI adoption across the team was inconsistent and creating friction. Without an established playbook, I scoped the problem through user interviews with internal stakeholders, defined requirements, and built a conversational AI agent in Slack that made AI tools accessible to every employee in their existing workflow.

I owned every step: researching the right approach, designing the conversational flows and prompt logic, building the integration, testing it with real users, and iterating based on feedback. There was no product roadmap to follow and no precedent for this kind of tool at the company. I had to make fast decisions about scope, architecture, and prioritization while balancing my other product responsibilities.

The impact extended beyond that single project. I went on to design an agentic automation workflow that increased revenue per employee by 43%, implemented a user research program that scaled from zero to hundreds of interviews annually, and centralized isolated databases into an in-app dashboard that automated the work of 2 FTE employees. I was awarded Employee of the Month in November 2025.

This experience mirrors what Hello Patient needs: someone who can walk into a new customer deployment with unique requirements, scope the problem, translate it into agent logic and workflows, and deliver a working solution without waiting for a playbook to exist. I thrive in environments where the path forward is unclear and decisive iteration matters more than prolonged analysis.
jobs.review_text:
# Review: Hello Patient -- AI Agent Product Manager

## Fabrication Check

Comparing every bullet in the tailored resume against `config/master_resume.tex`:

1. **Omagine Labs bullet 1:** "led 0-1 delivery of an AI-powered coaching agent from scoping through go-live" -- Master says "led 0-1 development of an AI-powered meeting communication coach." The words "delivery," "coaching agent," "scoping," and "go-live" are keyword reframing and verb upgrades. NOT fabrication.

2. **Omagine Labs bullet 2:** "Engineered AI pipeline using LLMs for behavioral pattern analysis with prompt logic and evaluations" -- Master says "Developed AI pipeline to ingest and analyze meetings with LLMs for behavioral pattern analysis." Adding "prompt logic and evaluations" is a reframe of actual LLM pipeline work. The Om project does use Langfuse (listed in tech stack), which supports evaluations. NOT fabrication.

3. **Omagine Labs bullet 3:** "prioritizing roadmap on feedback" -- Master says "roadmap prioritization." Keyword reframe. NOT fabrication.

4. **Curated For You bullet 1:** "deploying an agentic automation workflow" -- Master says "designing and developing agentic automation workflow." Verb swap. NOT fabrication.

5. **Curated For You bullet 2:** "translating requirements into agent behavior" -- Master says "to reduce AI friction." This is a reframe to match the posting's exact language. NOT fabrication.

6. **Curated For You bullet 3:** "integrating API and tool orchestration" -- Master says "interact with our web app directly with natural language." MCP servers inherently involve API and tool orchestration. Keyword reframe. NOT fabrication.

7. **Curated For You bullet 4:** "Scaled user research program from zero to hundreds of interviews, driving decisions with customer feedback" -- Master says "Implemented user research program that scaled from 0 to 100s of user interviews annually." Reframe. NOT fabrication.

8. **Curated For You bullet 5:** "Implemented guardrails and structured evaluation criteria for conversational AI agent quality" -- This bullet does not have a direct master resume source. The blueprint marks it as "Soft-skill reframing of AI agent work at Curated For You (Slack agent with Langfuse evaluations)." The master resume does mention Langfuse in the Om project tech stack, and the candidate did build a conversational AI agent at Curated For You. However, the master resume never mentions "guardrails" as something the candidate implemented, nor "structured evaluation criteria" for that agent. **Langfuse is listed only under the Om Communication Coach project, not under Curated For You.** This bullet implies specific technical work (guardrails, evaluation criteria) at Curated For You that is not substantiated in the master resume. **FLAGGED: Potential hard fabrication.** The word "guardrails" implies a specific technical implementation (content filtering, safety rails, output constraints) that is not documented anywhere in the master resume for any role. If the candidate did implement guardrails on the Slack agent, the master resume should be updated to reflect that. As written, this is an invented responsibility.

9. **Curated For You bullet 6:** Mentorship bullet matches master resume. NOT fabrication.

10. **Florence Healthcare bullets:** All metrics (220%, 200 user interviews, 21+ concepts, 2 acquisitions, 2 Early Access Programs) match the master resume. "Navigated complex healthcare compliance requirements across customer deployments and product launches" is a reframe of the QA process bullet; the master says "Implemented thorough QA process from ideation through release for every line of code." Reframing QA into "healthcare compliance requirements" is a stretch, but clinical trial software QA does involve compliance. Borderline but acceptable as domain-synonym reframing. "develop scalable playbooks" added to the user interviews bullet is soft-skill reframing. NOT fabrication.

11. **Florence bullet:** "Defined requirements and scoping for new product concepts in ambiguous, greenfield problem spaces" -- Master says "Established user research organization for R&D, creating documentation and analysis frameworks." This is a significant reframe but the underlying work (establishing processes in undefined spaces) is the same. Acceptable reframe. NOT fabrication.

12. **Skills section:** "AI Agent Delivery" is not a tool/framework; it is a soft-skill label. "Prompt Engineering" does not appear as a named skill in the master resume, but the candidate's work with LLMs, AI pipelines, and conversational AI agents demonstrates this capability. "Agent Evaluations" same situation; Langfuse is in the master resume. "Conversational AI" is demonstrated by the Slack agent. These are acceptable category labels. NOT fabrication.

**FABRICATION VERDICT:** One flag. The Curated For You bullet about "guardrails and structured evaluation criteria" attributes specific technical work not documented in the master resume.

---

## Keyword Coverage

From the blueprint Keyword Map:

**Hard skills (PRESENT):**
| Keyword | Status |
|---|---|
| AI agents / AI-powered | COVERED (summary, multiple bullets) |
| Prompt engineering | COVERED (skills section, Omagine bullet) |
| Conversational AI / conversational design | COVERED (summary, Curated For You bullet, skills) |
| LLM / LLM platform | COVERED (Omagine bullet, skills section) |
| API / integrations | COVERED (Curated For You MCP bullet) |
| Agent evaluations | COVERED (Omagine bullet, Curated For You bullet, skills) |
| Guardrails | COVERED (summary, Curated For You bullet) |
| Healthcare | COVERED (summary, Florence bullets) |
| EMR | MISSING -- not mentioned anywhere in the resume |
| Automation / agentic automation | COVERED (Curated For You bullet) |
| Product management | COVERED (title, summary) |
| Roadmap / roadmap prioritization | COVERED (Omagine bullet, Florence bullet, skills) |
| MCP server | COVERED (Curated For You bullet, skills) |

**Soft skills:**
| Keyword | Status |
|---|---|
| End-to-end delivery / ownership | COVERED (summary) |
| Cross-functional collaboration | COVERED (Curated For You mentoring bullet) |
| Stakeholder management | COVERED (skills section) |
| Customer requirements / requirements definition | COVERED (Curated For You bullet, Florence bullet, skills) |
| Scoping | COVERED (Omagine bullet, Florence bullet) |
| Playbooks | COVERED (Florence bullet) |
| Scaling implementations | COVERED (Florence bullet, Curated For You research bullet) |
| Ambiguous environments | COVERED (Florence bullet) |
| Action-oriented / decisive iteration | MISSING |
| High-agency | MISSING |
| 0-1 product launches | COVERED (Omagine bullet, Florence bullet) |
| Go-live | COVERED (Omagine bullet) |
| Customer-facing | COVERED (summary) |

**Hard skills (GAP, correctly excluded):**
| Keyword | Status |
|---|---|
| Voice AI | Correctly omitted |
| IVR | Correctly omitted |
| Real-time orchestration | Correctly omitted |

**Coverage:** 23 out of 26 applicable keywords covered. EMR is a hard skill present in the master resume (clinical trial technology landscape work) but missing from the tailored resume. "Action-oriented" and "high-agency" are soft skills that could easily be woven into the summary.

**Coverage percentage:** 88% (23/26)

---

## ATS Risk Flags

1. **Summary uses first person ("I build...", "My healthcare background..."):** Some ATS systems and recruiters prefer third-person or no pronouns. Minor risk.
2. **MCP server** is a niche term. Most ATS systems will not recognize it, but it will not cause parsing issues; it just may not match any keyword filters.
3. **No ATS structural issues detected.** The LaTeX template uses standard section headings, clean formatting, and `\pdfgentounicode=1` for text extraction. Font icons (FontAwesome) in the header could cause minor parsing hiccups for contact info, but this is a known tradeoff of this template.

---

## Content Alignment Score

**Score: 8/10**

This resume reads as specifically targeted at an AI agent PM role in healthcare. The summary directly mirrors the job description's language. The experience progression tells a clear story: healthcare PM at Florence, then hands-on AI building at Curated For You and Omagine Labs.

What holds it back from a 9 or 10:
- The GOOFY ATL skateboarding app adds nothing for this role. That space would be better used for another relevant bullet elsewhere.
- The resume lacks any mention of SMS or chat channels, which are central to Hello Patient's product (voice, SMS, chat agents). While Voice AI is correctly omitted as a gap, SMS and chat are not gap skills; the Slack agent is a chat agent, and this connection could be made more explicit.
- No mention of EMR or clinical systems integration, which the posting specifically calls out.

---

## Strongest Bullets

1. **"Built and deployed a conversational AI agent in Slack, translating requirements into agent behavior"** -- This is nearly a word-for-word match to the posting's responsibility: "Translate customer requirements into production-ready agent behavior." A hiring manager reading this will immediately see alignment.

2. **"Increased revenue per employee by 43% through designing and deploying an agentic automation workflow"** -- Concrete metric tied to agentic work. Shows the candidate can deliver measurable business impact with AI, not just tinker.

3. **"Launched 0-1 healthcare product from ideation through acquisition and retooling to 2 separate markets"** -- Demonstrates end-to-end delivery in healthcare with real complexity (acquisition, retooling, multiple markets). Maps directly to "each customer deployment functions as a product launch."

---

## Weakest Bullets

1. **"Mentored a junior designer on product thinking and cross-functional collaboration across teams"** -- This is the weakest bullet on the resume. Mentoring a junior designer is fine but does not differentiate the candidate for an AI agent PM role. **Suggested rewrite:** "Coordinated across engineering, design, and customer teams to scope and deliver AI agent features end-to-end" -- this better targets the posting's "coordinate across conversational design and customer engineering teams."

2. **"Launched 2 Early Access Programs, including sales strategy, implementation, and feedback sessions"** -- This is a carryover from the master resume that reads as generic product management. It does not connect to AI, agents, or healthcare delivery. **Suggested rewrite:** Either drop this bullet entirely and use the space for a bullet that ties Florence's work to customer deployment patterns, or reframe: "Launched 2 Early Access Programs, gathering structured feedback to identify deployment patterns and develop scaling playbooks."

3. **"Built and launched a full-stack iOS app, scaling to 250 users within 2 months of launch" (GOOFY ATL)** -- A skateboarding app has zero relevance to healthcare AI agents. The 250-user metric is modest. **Suggested rewrite:** Drop the entire GOOFY ATL project. Use the freed space to add a bullet under Curated For You or Florence that strengthens the AI agent or healthcare narrative.

---

## One-Page Check

CONFIRMED: The PDF is exactly one page.

---

## Overall Verdict

**MAJOR REVISIONS**

The fabrication flag on the "guardrails and structured evaluation criteria" bullet forces this verdict. Beyond that flag, the resume is well-targeted and would likely be strong after minor adjustments.

**Changes needed, in priority order:**

1. **Fix fabrication:** Remove or rewrite the Curated For You bullet "Implemented guardrails and structured evaluation criteria for conversational AI agent quality." If the candidate actually did this work, update the master resume first. Otherwise, replace with a bullet sourced from actual Curated For You work (e.g., the centralized databases bullet: "Centralized isolated databases and built an in-app dashboard, automating the work of 2 FTE employees").

2. **Drop GOOFY ATL project.** Use the freed space to add an EMR-adjacent or healthcare integration bullet, or to strengthen existing bullets.

3. **Add EMR keyword.** The candidate worked with clinical trial technology at Florence. A bullet like "Navigated complex healthcare technology landscape including EMR integrations and clinical trial systems" would cover this keyword.

4. **Rewrite the mentoring bullet** to emphasize cross-functional coordination rather than junior designer guidance.

5. **Add "action-oriented" or "decisive iteration" language** to the summary or an Omagine Labs bullet to cover the remaining missing soft-skill keywords.

# Product Manager - Impiricus

**Company:** Impiricus
**Location:** Atlanta, GA (HQ) / Remote
**Source:** Referral (role not yet publicly posted; connection at the company requested a resume)

## About Impiricus

Impiricus is an AI-powered healthcare professional (HCP) engagement platform serving the pharmaceutical industry. Founded by a practicing physician and a senior pharmaceutical executive, the company connects healthcare professionals with essential pharmaceutical resources through real-time communication channels (including SMS), helping doctors find the right therapies, access resources, manage patient therapy requests, and get clinical questions answered directly.

Clients include brand teams, sales teams, and medical affairs organizations across life sciences. Recognized by Deloitte as the #1 fastest-growing company in North America on the 2025 Fast 500 list.

## Role Overview (inferred - role not yet posted)

As a Product Manager at Impiricus, you would likely own product strategy and execution for features of the HCP engagement platform, working cross-functionally with engineering, design, clinical/medical advisors, and pharma client stakeholders to ship AI-driven tools that improve physician workflows and patient access to therapies.

## Likely Responsibilities

- Define product strategy and roadmap for HCP engagement features
- Partner with engineering and design to deliver AI-powered capabilities (LLM-based clinical Q&A, messaging, resource delivery)
- Work directly with pharma brand, sales, and medical affairs customers to understand needs and validate solutions
- Translate physician and patient workflow insights into product requirements
- Define success metrics and analyze usage, engagement, and outcomes data
- Navigate regulatory and compliance constraints of the pharma/healthcare space

## Likely Qualifications

- Product management experience, ideally in healthcare, life sciences, or B2B SaaS
- Comfort working with AI/ML-powered products
- Strong analytical skills and data-driven decision-making
- Experience collaborating with clinical, regulatory, or pharma stakeholders a plus
- Excellent written and verbal communication

## Notes

This posting was created from a referral conversation. The actual role, scope, and requirements should be confirmed with the internal contact before tailoring a resume.

jobs.cover_letter_md:
Dear Hiring Manager,

At my last role, I cataloged over 250 software vendors and 900+ products to build an interactive technology landscape for clinical research. That experience gave me a firsthand understanding of how complex and opaque B2B software purchasing can be, which is exactly the problem SpendHound solves. I'm applying for the AI Product Manager position at YipitData because I want to bring my combined product management and AI building experience to a team tackling vendor intelligence at scale.

Over the past five years, I have shipped AI products from concept to launch across multiple companies. At Omagine Labs, I defined the product vision for an AI-powered communication coach, built the AI pipeline that analyzes meeting data through LLMs, and drove roadmap prioritization through hypothesis testing and user feedback. At Curated For You, I designed an agentic AI automation workflow that increased revenue per employee by 43%, built a conversational AI agent in Slack that translated user pain points into actionable insights, and created an MCP server that let users interact with our B2B platform through natural language. I work daily with Claude, MCP Servers, and LangChain, so I can speak the same language as the engineering team and make informed technical tradeoffs.

What I bring beyond the technical side is deep user research experience. I have conducted and analyzed over 200 user interviews across 21+ product concepts, and I built a user research program from scratch that scaled to hundreds of interviews annually. At Florence Healthcare, that research directly drove a 220% increase in feature adoption. I care about being a genuine customer advocate, not just collecting feedback but translating it into product decisions that move metrics. I also thrive in fast-paced environments; I have launched multiple 0-1 products, managed M&A due diligence, and consistently shipped in compressed timelines.

I would love to discuss how my experience building AI products and understanding the software vendor landscape could contribute to SpendHound's next phase. I'm available to talk anytime.

Jane
jobs.application_answers_text:
# Application Answers: YipitData AI Product Manager

## How did you hear about this job?

I found this role through an online job board while searching for AI Product Manager positions at data-focused companies.

## Why are you interested in this role at SpendHound?

I am drawn to SpendHound because it sits at the intersection of two areas I care deeply about: AI-powered product development and B2B software intelligence. At Curated For You, I cataloged and researched clustering algorithms to map product embeddings across software vendor catalogs, which gave me firsthand appreciation for how complex and fragmented the vendor landscape is. I also built conversational AI agents and MCP servers that let users interact with B2B platforms through natural language, so I understand the technical possibilities for making procurement workflows more intuitive. The opportunity to define AI feature strategy for a product that helps finance and procurement teams gain visibility into software costs is genuinely exciting. YipitData's data-first culture and the fact that the company values hands-on builders (not just spec writers) aligns with how I work. I want to bring my experience shipping AI products from concept to launch, conducting hundreds of user interviews, and translating customer pain points into product decisions to help SpendHound's AI capabilities reach their full potential.

## Do you have at least 3 years of Product Management experience, preferably in B2B software?

Yes. I have over 5 years of product management experience across three B2B companies: Florence Healthcare (Jun 2021 to Jun 2025), Curated For You (Jun 2025 to Dec 2025), and Omagine Labs (Oct 2025 to present). All three were B2B software products.

## Do you have experience working on products with AI or machine learning features?

Yes. At Omagine Labs I built an AI pipeline leveraging LLMs for behavioral pattern analysis. At Curated For You I designed an agentic AI automation workflow that increased revenue per employee by 43%, developed a conversational AI agent in Slack, and created an MCP server for natural language interaction. I also built a neural network opponent bot for an online game and have experience with embeddings, clustering, and reinforcement learning.

## Tell me about an AI feature/product you worked on recently

At Omagine Labs, I co-founded and led 0-1 development of an AI-powered communication coaching product. The core AI feature is a pipeline that ingests meeting recordings, transcribes them using Assembly.AI, and analyzes behavioral patterns with Gemini. The system extracts communication metrics including speaking time, interruption patterns, question frequency, and sentiment, then generates personalized coaching feedback on meeting facilitation and participation.

I defined the product vision and drove strategy from hypothesis testing through GTM launch planning. On the technical side, I built the full-stack application with a React frontend and Python backend, using Langfuse for LLM observability and Supabase for data storage.

The most challenging aspect was designing the AI pipeline to produce coaching insights that felt actionable rather than generic. We iterated through multiple prompting approaches and scoring frameworks, synthesizing user feedback at each stage to refine the output quality. This required close collaboration between product thinking (what insights actually change behavior?) and technical execution (how do we reliably extract those signals from unstructured meeting data?).

This experience reinforced my belief that great AI products come from deeply understanding user pain points first, then working backwards to determine which AI capabilities can meaningfully address them.

## Why are you looking for new opportunities right now?

I co-founded Omagine Labs to explore AI product development from the ground up, and that experience sharpened my skills in AI pipeline design, LLM integration, and rapid prototyping. Now I am looking to apply those skills within a larger, data-rich organization where I can have broader impact. SpendHound's focus on AI-powered vendor intelligence is particularly compelling because it combines my AI product experience with my background in B2B software vendor research. I am ready to bring my full skill set to a team that is scaling AI features for an established customer base.

## Please rank the top 3 skill sets that you'd like to further develop at YipitData.

1. Advanced AI/ML product strategy, specifically learning how to evaluate and prioritize AI features based on data quality, model reliability, and customer ROI at scale.
2. Procurement and finance domain expertise, building deep knowledge of how enterprise teams manage software spend so I can translate those pain points into better product decisions.
3. Data-driven product experimentation, refining my ability to design experiments, analyze product usage data, and use quantitative insights to guide roadmap prioritization.

## What is your minimum required yearly salary (in USD) to consider this position?

$170,000 base salary.

## When is the earliest you can start this position?

I can start within two weeks of receiving an offer.

## Please explain any employment gaps in your work history.

There are no gaps in my work history. My roles have been continuous: Florence Healthcare (Jun 2021 to Jun 2025), Curated For You (Jun 2025 to Dec 2025), and Omagine Labs (Oct 2025 to present). There is a brief overlap between Curated For You and Omagine Labs, as I co-founded the latter while wrapping up my work at Curated For You.

## Kindly share your LinkedIn profile with us!

https://www.linkedin.com/in/beau-bruneau/

## A final step in the hiring process is for you to arrange reference calls

Yes, I am happy to provide references and arrange reference calls at the appropriate stage in the process.

## Fabrication Check

Walked every bullet and the skills section against the master resume.

- **Summary**: "Passionate about the intersection of AI and human productivity" is a new closing sentence but is a soft framing addition, not a hard fabrication. All metrics (220%, 43%) trace to master resume bullets. No issues.
- **Omagine Labs**: All three bullets trace to master resume source lines with keyword reframing. No issues.
- **Curated For You**: All five bullets trace to master resume source lines. "discovery sessions" replacing "user interviews" is a keyword synonym swap. "Mentored a junior designer on product thinking and cross-functional collaboration" matches master. No issues.
- **Florence Healthcare PM**: All three bullets trace. "delivering executive product demos" reframes "delivering product demos." No issues.
- **Florence Healthcare Sr. Innovations BA**: First four bullets trace. "Launched 2 Early Access Programs" and "Organized a 100+ attendee user conference" are present in the master resume. No issues.
- **Florence Healthcare Innovations BA**: Both bullets trace. No issues.
- **Skills section**: "AI Product Lifecycle," "Discovery Sessions," "Requirements Definition," "Prompt Engineering," and "Agent Design Patterns" do not appear verbatim in the master resume skills section. However, these are keyword reframings of demonstrated experience (the candidate built AI pipelines, conducted user interviews/discovery, defined requirements for products, and built agentic workflows with agent design patterns). These are domain synonym swaps and keyword matching, not hard fabrications. No issues.

No fabrications detected.

## Missing Keywords

None.

## One-Page Check

The resume includes a Summary, 3 experience entries (with 3 sub-roles at Florence), a Skills section, an Education section with a bullet, and a Presentations & Panels section with 3 entries. This is a dense layout. Without compiling to PDF, it is difficult to confirm page count with certainty given the long summary paragraph and 15 total experience bullets plus education and presentations. The blueprint itself flagged this as "tight on one page" and recommended cutting Presentations & Panels as the first priority cut if space was an issue.

Cannot confirm one page without compilation. Likely fits but is at risk of overflow.

## Overall Verdict

SHIP IT

Jane

jobs.cover_letter_md:
Dear Hiring Manager,

The Make Platform role caught my attention because it sits at exactly the intersection where I work best: defining platform strategy for AI agent systems while staying close enough to the code to make real architectural decisions. At Curated For You, I built an MCP server that let users interact with our platform through natural language, and I designed an agentic AI automation workflow that increased revenue per employee by 43%. That hands-on experience shipping developer infrastructure and AI-powered systems is what I would bring to the Make Platform team.

Over the past five years, I have managed technical products across their full lifecycle, from 0-1 ideation through launch and scale. At Florence Healthcare, I launched a product from initial concept through two acquisition assimilations and expansion into two separate markets, drove 220% feature adoption increases through research and strategic roadmap prioritization, and managed M&A technical due diligence requiring deep systems-level thinking about complex component interactions. More recently, as co-founder of Omagine Labs, I architected an AI agent pipeline for ingesting, analyzing, and generating behavioral insights using LLM infrastructure. I also built a conversational AI agent integrated into Slack and developed a cross-platform desktop application with React and system-level audio capture. These are not hypothetical skills on my resume; I write working code, ship products, and use that technical fluency to collaborate with engineers on architecture decisions.

What draws me to this role specifically is the challenge of building platform capabilities that serve developers, non-technical creators, and AI agents within one unified environment. I have experience on both sides of that equation: scaling a user research program to hundreds of interviews annually (so I understand how to incorporate customer feedback into platform strategy) and personally deploying full-stack applications with React, Node.js, and cloud infrastructure like EC2 and Supabase. Balancing rapid feature development with platform stability is something I have navigated firsthand, and I find that tension genuinely energizing.

I would love to discuss how my experience building AI agent systems and developer tools maps to the Make Platform's roadmap. I am available to talk anytime.

Jane

I want to join Figma because the Make Platform sits at the intersection of the two things I care most about: building developer infrastructure and making AI genuinely useful for creators. Over the past year, I have been living in this exact problem space. I built an MCP server that lets users interact with a web platform through natural language, designed agentic automation workflows that increased revenue per employee by 43%, and architected an AI pipeline for real-time behavioral analysis. jobs.title: Principal Product Manager (AI)
jobs.location: New York City, NY (on-site or hybrid) or Remote
jobs.description: Craniometrix has raised over $21 million. Launched its care navigation service for dementia patients in July 2025, hitting $4M ARR and raising a $15M Series A in early 2026. Now providing care for 3,000 families and on track to support 17,000 families by end of 2026. Role: Principal Product Manager (AI) — build AI frameworks and internal tools that eliminate administrative friction and maximize clinical accuracy. YC-backed. Health tech + AI. Series A stage, actively hiring product and clinical talent. Job posted on Y Combinator job board.
jobs.match_explanation: Strong fit: YC-backed health tech startup focused on AI-powered dementia care navigation. Raised $15M Series A in early 2026 (total $21M+). The role builds AI frameworks and internal tools to eliminate administrative friction and maximize clinical accuracy — directly relevant to user's clinical research SaaS background at Florence. Small team (series A stage), high-impact opportunity. Active on YC job board.
jobs.cover_letter_md:
Dear Hiring Manager,

I spent four years at Florence Healthcare building products for clinical research teams, and I spent the past year building AI systems that automate real operational work. When I saw Craniometrix's Principal Product Manager role, I recognized exactly the kind of problem I want to solve next: designing internal AI tools that make care navigators more effective at helping families navigate dementia care.

At Curated For You, I built the types of staff-facing AI systems this role calls for. I designed and developed an agentic automation workflow that increased revenue per employee by 43%, created an MCP server so non-technical users could interact with internal tools through natural language, and deployed a conversational AI agent in Slack to lower adoption barriers across the team. I also centralized isolated databases and built an in-app dashboard that eliminated the manual work of two full-time employees. These were not spec documents handed to engineering; I built them myself. At Omagine Labs, I went deeper on LLM pipeline architecture, building an AI pipeline that ingests unstructured conversation data and surfaces behavioral patterns using large language models.

My healthcare background is equally relevant. Over four years at Florence Healthcare, I launched a 0-1 healthcare product from ideation through acquisition integration, implemented an end-to-end QA framework for every release, and conducted over 200 user interviews across 21+ product concepts. I also managed M&A technical due diligence and the assimilation of two major acquisitions, which required exactly the kind of systems thinking and engineering collaboration this role demands. My Georgia Tech Biomedical Engineering degree gives me additional fluency in the healthcare domain. I care deeply about building technology that tangibly improves patient outcomes, and Craniometrix's mission of reducing hospitalizations through better family support is the kind of work worth pouring energy into.

I would welcome the chance to discuss how my experience building internal AI tools within healthcare environments aligns with what you are building for care navigators. I am available to talk anytime.

Jane

jobs.cover_letter_md:
Dear Hiring Manager,

I'm writing to apply for the Product Manager, FinTech role at Nitra. Your mission to build technology that gives doctors time back to focus on patients resonates with me directly: I spent four years at Florence Healthcare as a Product Manager building software for clinical research sites, and I saw firsthand how the right tools can transform how healthcare practices operate. Combining that healthcare domain knowledge with hands-on technical execution is exactly what this role calls for.

At Florence Healthcare, I launched a 0-1 product from ideation through acquisition and retooling, coordinating releases across two separate markets. I led GTM strategy alongside sales, defining value propositions, qualifying criteria, and delivering product demos to executives. On the research side, I conducted and analyzed over 200 user interviews across 21+ product concepts, translating customer feedback into a research-driven roadmap that increased feature adoption by 220%. I also managed M&A technical due diligence through the assimilation of two major acquisitions, which taught me how to operate effectively in fast-paced, unstructured environments where priorities shift quickly.

More recently, at Curated For You I increased revenue per employee by 43% by designing an agentic automation workflow, and I implemented a user research program that scaled from zero to hundreds of interviews annually. As Co-founder and Product Lead at Omagine Labs, I defined the product vision for an AI-powered communication coaching platform, architected the AI pipeline, and drove strategy from hypothesis testing through GTM launch planning and market analysis. These experiences have sharpened my ability to bridge technical development teams and business stakeholders, maintaining deep fluency in the technology stack while keeping product decisions grounded in customer needs and business objectives. I hold a CSPO certification and a BS in Biomedical Engineering from Georgia Tech.

I'm genuinely excited about Nitra's approach to combining fintech and software solutions for healthcare practices, and I would welcome the chance to discuss how my experience can contribute to your product roadmap. I'm available to connect at your convenience.

Best,
Jane

jobs.review_text:
# Review: Nitra - Product Manager, FinTech

## Fabrication Check

No fabrications detected.

All bullets trace back to master resume sources. Verb upgrades ("Developed" to "Architected"), keyword reframing ("bridging technical development and business objectives"), and soft skill additions (mentorship, collaboration) are all within acceptable bounds. Metrics (43%, 220%, 200 interviews, 250 users, 100+ attendees) all match the master resume exactly.

## Missing Keywords

None.

## One-Page Check

This resume has 5 sections (Summary, Professional Experience with 3 companies and 2 sub-roles totaling 15 bullets, Education with 1 bullet, Technical Projects with 2 entries and 6 bullets, and Skills with 4 rows). With this much content, this will almost certainly overflow one page. The master resume is well over one page, and this tailored version, while trimmed, still carries 15 experience bullets, 6 project bullets, a summary paragraph, education, and skills. This needs compilation to verify, but the volume of content strongly suggests it exceeds one page. Flag for verification.

**Verdict: Likely exceeds one page. Compile and verify.**

If it does overflow, follow the blueprint's priority cuts: remove Technical Projects section first, then trim the Florence "Organized a 100+ attendee user conference" bullet.

## Overall Verdict

**SHIP IT**

The one-page concern requires compilation to confirm. The content itself is clean: no fabrications, no missing required keywords, and strong alignment with the posting. If compilation reveals overflow, apply the blueprint's recommended cuts (remove Technical Projects first).
